   584→These are contradictory.
   585→
   586→ANALYSIS:
   587→  - 35% annual discount for Builder is aggressive. It increases
   588→    annual conversion but sacrifices $41/year per subscriber.
   589→    At 100 annual subscribers, that is $4,100 in lost revenue.
   590→  - 16.7% (current) is industry-standard for B2B SaaS.
   591→  - 17% for Enterprise (Strategy) is nearly identical to current.
   592→
   593→RECOMMENDATION: Keep current 16.7% discount (2 months free).
   594→This is the industry standard and the codebase already implements
   595→it correctly. The Strategy doc's 35% Builder discount was likely
   596→meant as a promotional launch price, not a permanent structure.
   597→```
   598→
   599→---
   600→
   601→## 8. Infrastructure Cost Model
   602→
   603→### 8.1 Cost at Scale Milestones
   604→
   605→```
   606→+------------------+--------+--------+--------+--------+---------+
   607→| Cost Item        |  100   |  500   | 1,000  | 5,000  | 10,000  |
   608→|                  | users  | users  | users  | users  | users   |
   609→+------------------+--------+--------+--------+--------+---------+
   610→| Railway API      |  $5.00 | $10.00 | $20.00 | $50.00 | $100.00 |
   611→| Railway Sched.   |  $5.00 |  $5.00 |  $5.00 |  $5.00 |  $10.00 |
   612→| Vercel           |  $0.00 |  $0.00 | $20.00 | $20.00 |  $20.00 |
   613→| Supabase DB      |  $0.00 | $25.00 | $25.00 | $25.00 |  $75.00 |
   614→| Redis (Railway)  |  $0.00 |  $5.00 |  $5.00 | $10.00 |  $20.00 |
   615→| Anthropic API    |  $2.10 |  $2.10 |  $4.00 |  $6.00 |  $10.00 |
   616→| OpenAI embed.    |  $0.15 |  $0.15 |  $0.15 |  $0.15 |   $0.15 |
   617→| Resend email     |  $0.00 |  $0.00 | $20.00 | $20.00 |  $20.00 |
   618→| SerpAPI/Brave    |  $0.00 |  $0.00 |  $5.00 | $50.00 |  $50.00 |
   619→| Sentry           |  $0.00 |  $0.00 | $26.00 | $26.00 |  $26.00 |
   620→| Domain + DNS     |  $1.50 |  $1.50 |  $1.50 |  $1.50 |   $1.50 |
   621→+------------------+--------+--------+--------+--------+---------+
   622→| TOTAL INFRA/mo   | $13.75 | $48.75 |$131.65 |$213.65 | $332.65 |
   623→+------------------+--------+--------+--------+--------+---------+
   624→| Revenue/mo (est) |$114.00 |$760.00 |$2,375  |$11,875 | $23,750 |
   625→| Infra % of rev   |  12.1% |   6.4% |   5.5% |   1.8% |    1.4% |
   626→+------------------+--------+--------+--------+--------+---------+
   627→
   628→Revenue assumptions: 8% paid, 90% Builder / 10% Enterprise,
   629→blended ARPU ~$26/mo per paid user.
   630→
   631→KEY INSIGHT: Infrastructure costs scale sub-linearly. The
   632→pipeline runs once regardless of user count. The primary
   633→scaling bottlenecks are:
   634→  1. Email delivery (sequential, 1/sec) -- 10,000 users =
   635→     ~2.8 hours to send. May need batch sending optimization.
   636→  2. Database queries -- archive/search at scale needs
   637→     pagination (already noted in plan review).
   638→  3. Railway API -- may need horizontal scaling at 5,000+ users.
   639→```
   640→
   641→### 8.2 Marginal Cost Per Additional User
   642→
   643→```
   644→+-------------------------+----------+
   645→| Cost Component          | Per User |
   646→+-------------------------+----------+
   647→| Pipeline AI (shared)    |   ~$0.00 |
   648→| Email delivery          |   ~$0.02 |
   649→| Database storage        |   ~$0.01 |
   650→| API serving             |   ~$0.01 |
   651→| Stripe processing       |   ~$0.85 |  (Builder Monthly)
   652→+-------------------------+----------+
   653→| Total marginal cost     |   ~$0.89 |
   654→| As % of $19 revenue     |     4.7% |
   655→+-------------------------+----------+
   656→
   657→The marginal cost of serving an additional user is dominated
   658→entirely by Stripe processing fees. All other costs are either
   659→shared (pipeline) or negligible (email, storage).
   660→```
   661→
   662→---
   663→
   664→## 9. Comparable Company Benchmarks
   665→
   666→### 9.1 Industry Take Rates and Pricing Benchmarks
   667→
   668→```
   669→+---------------------+----------+----------+------------+---------+
   670→| Company             | Entry $  | Top $    | Model      | Gross   |
   671→|                     |          |          |            | Margin  |
   672→+---------------------+----------+----------+------------+---------+
   673→| Exploding Topics    |  $39/mo  | $249/mo  | Subscr.    | ~90%    |
   674→| SparkToro           |  $38/mo  | $112/mo  | Subscr.    | ~85%    |
   675→| Glimpse             |  $99/mo  | $99+/mo  | Subscr.    | ~90%    |
   676→| BuzzSumo            | $199/mo  | $999/mo  | Subscr.    | ~85%    |
   677→| Brandwatch          | $800+/mo | Custom   | Subscr.    | ~80%    |
   678→| BigIdeasDB          |  $20/mo  |    N/A   | Subscr.    | ~95%    |
   679→| GummySearch (disc.) |  $29/mo  | $199/mo  | Subscr.    | ~90%    |
   680→+---------------------+----------+----------+------------+---------+
   681→| ZeroToShip          |  $19/mo  |  $99/mo  | Subscr.    | ~95%+   |
   682→+---------------------+----------+----------+------------+---------+
   683→
   684→ZeroToShip has the lowest entry price and the highest gross margin
   685→in its competitive set, primarily because AI pipeline costs are
   686→near-zero on a per-user basis.
   687→```
   688→
   689→### 9.2 SaaS Benchmark Comparison
   690→
   691→```
   692→+---------------------------+-----------+---------------+
   693→| Metric                    | Industry  | ZeroToShip    |
   694→|                           | Benchmark | (Projected)   |
   695→+---------------------------+-----------+---------------+
   696→| Gross margin              | 70-85%    | 95%+          |
   697→| LTV:CAC (organic)         | 3:1       | 177:1         |
   698→| LTV:CAC (paid)            | 3:1       | 18:1          |
   699→| CAC payback (months)      | 6-18      | <1            |
   700→| Monthly churn (SMB)       | 3-7%      | 5-6% (est.)   |
   701→| Annual retention (SMB)    | 60-75%    | ~52% (est.)*  |
   702→| Net revenue retention     | 90-110%   | ~95% (est.)** |
   703→| ARPU                      | Varies    | $18-94        |
   704→| Revenue per employee      | $150-250K | N/A (solo)    |
   705→+---------------------------+-----------+---------------+
   706→
   707→* 6% monthly churn = 52% annual retention. This is below
   708→  benchmark but expected for early-stage, low-price SaaS.
   709→  Improving retention is the highest-leverage growth action.
   710→
   711→** No expansion revenue mechanism exists yet. Adding usage-based
   712→   pricing, add-ons (validation at $79/idea), or tier upgrades
   713→   would improve NRR significantly.
   714→```
   715→
   716→### 9.3 Stripe Fee Benchmarks
   717→
   718→```
   719→Standard Stripe pricing (US):
   720→  - Online payments: 2.9% + $0.30
   721→  - International cards: +1.5%
   722→  - Currency conversion: +1.0%
   723→  - Recurring billing: Included in standard rate
   724→  - Stripe Tax: +0.5% per transaction (if enabled)
   725→  - Stripe Radar (fraud): Free for basic, $0.02/txn for advanced
   726→  - Chargeback fee: $15.00
   727→
   728→Effective Stripe take rate by price point:
   729→  $19/mo:  4.5%   ($0.85 per charge)
   730→  $99/mo:  3.2%   ($3.17 per charge)
   731→  $190/yr: 3.1%   ($5.80 per charge)
   732→  $990/yr: 2.9%   ($29.00 per charge)
   733→
   734→Stripe is non-negotiable at this volume. Custom pricing
   735→typically begins at $100K+/month in processing volume.
   736→```
   737→
   738→---
   739→
   740→## 10. Key Assumptions & Sensitivity
   741→
   742→### The 3 Assumptions That Matter Most
   743→
   744→```
   745→+---+------------------------+----------+----------+----------+
   746→| # | Assumption             | Bear     | Base     | Bull     |
   747→+---+------------------------+----------+----------+----------+
   748→| 1 | Free-to-paid conv. %   |    5%    |   10%    |   15%    |
   749→| 2 | Monthly churn rate     |    8%    |    6%    |    5%    |
   750→| 3 | Organic growth sustain.|  150/mo  |  500/mo  | 1,500/mo |
   751→+---+------------------------+----------+----------+----------+
   752→
   753→Impact analysis (Month 12 MRR sensitivity):
   754→
   755→  Conversion rate +1%:  +$950/mo MRR   (high leverage)
   756→  Churn rate -1%:       +$1,800/mo MRR (highest leverage)
   757→  Growth +100 users/mo: +$380/mo MRR   (moderate leverage)
   758→
   759→CHURN IS THE HIGHEST-LEVERAGE VARIABLE. Reducing monthly
   760→churn from 6% to 4% increases Month 12 MRR by ~$3,600 and
   761→doubles LTV. This should be the primary product focus
   762→after achieving initial traction.
   763→```
   764→
   765→### Sensitivity Matrix: Month 12 MRR
   766→
   767→```
   768→                    Churn Rate
   769→                 8%       6%       4%       3%
   770→Conv.  5%    |  $1,133 | $1,500 | $2,100 | $2,600 |
   771→Rate  10%    |  $2,266 | $3,000 | $4,200 | $5,200 |
   772→      15%    |  $3,399 | $4,500 | $6,300 | $7,800 |
   773→
   774→(Based on 500 signups/month steady state, Builder only)
   775→
   776→The sweet spot is the diagonal: 10% conversion + 4% churn
   777→yields ~$4,200 MRR at Month 12, which is in the base-to-bull
   778→range of the break-even model.
   779→```
   780→
   781→---
   782→
   783→## 11. Recommendations
   784→
   785→### 11.1 Pricing Actions (Priority Order)
   786→
   787→1. **HOLD Builder at $19/mo** through Month 6. The price is strategically
   788→   correct -- below the $20 ChatGPT anchor, undercutting all competitors,
   789→   and the conversion curve is nearly flat between $19-24. Do not change
   790→   this price until product-market fit is validated.
   791→
   792→2. **INCREASE Enterprise to $149/mo at Month 3-4**. The market supports
   793→   $112-249. Add Slack integration and keyword monitoring first to justify
   794→   the increase. Grandfather early Enterprise customers at $99.
   795→
   796→3. **RESOLVE annual pricing discrepancy**. The codebase implements 16.7%
   797→   annual discount ($190/yr Builder, $990/yr Enterprise). The Strategy doc
   798→   recommends 35% / 17%. Pick one and update the other. Recommendation:
   799→   keep the codebase's 16.7% discount -- it is industry-standard and more
   800→   revenue-optimal.
   801→
   802→4. **IMPLEMENT the validation add-on at $79/idea**. The schema and route
   803→   infrastructure exist. This is pure incremental revenue at near-zero
   804→   marginal cost ($0.05-0.15 in Claude API calls per validation).
   805→
   806→5. **CONSIDER a $24/mo price test at Month 6**. If retention data shows
   807→   <5% monthly churn by then, a $5 increase adds ~$2,500/mo at 500 paid
   808→   users with minimal conversion impact.
   809→
   810→### 11.2 Cost Optimization Actions
   811→
   812→1. **Monitor Stripe fees as % of revenue**. At current scale, Stripe is
   813→   the largest cost center (4.5% of Builder revenue). No action needed now,
   814→   but at $100K+/mo processing, negotiate custom Stripe pricing.
   815→
   816→2. **Stay on free tiers as long as possible**. Supabase, Vercel, Resend,
   817→   PostHog, and SerpAPI all have free tiers that cover the first 100-500
   818→   users. Do not upgrade until limits are hit.
   819→
   820→3. **Pipeline cost is not a concern**. At $2.10/month, it is irrelevant
   821→   to the P&L. Do not over-optimize here -- focus engineering time on
   822→   product quality and retention instead.
   823→
   824→4. **Plan for email scaling**. At 1,000+ subscribers, sequential email
   825→   sending at 1/second takes ~17 minutes per batch. At 5,000+ subscribers,
   826→   this becomes 83 minutes. Consider upgrading to Resend Pro ($20/mo) and
   827→   increasing concurrency/batch strategies before hitting 500 users.
   828→
   829→### 11.3 Growth & Retention Priorities
   830→
   831→1. **Reducing churn from 6% to 4% doubles LTV**. Build switching costs:
   832→   saved ideas, preferences, usage history, integrations. This is the
   833→   single highest-leverage financial action.
   834→
   835→2. **Net Revenue Retention is currently ~95%** (no expansion). Adding
   836→   usage-based pricing (API calls, validation add-ons, spec generation
   837→   tiers) would create expansion revenue and push NRR above 100%.
   838→
   839→3. **Free trial not yet implemented**. The Strategy doc recommends a
   840→   7-day Pro trial. This is not configured in Stripe. Adding it would
   841→   likely improve conversion by 2-5 percentage points based on industry
   842→   benchmarks.
   843→
   844→---
   845→
   846→## 12. Key Risks
   847→
   848→### Financial Risks
   849→
   850→```
   851→+---+-----------------------------------+--------+--------+------------------+
   852→| # | Risk                              | Prob.  | Impact | Mitigation       |
   853→+---+-----------------------------------+--------+--------+------------------+
   854→| 1 | Free-to-paid conversion <5%      | Medium | HIGH   | A/B test pricing,|
   855→|   |                                   |        |        | add free trial,  |
   856→|   |                                   |        |        | blurred content  |
   857→+---+-----------------------------------+--------+--------+------------------+
   858→| 2 | Monthly churn >8%                | Medium | HIGH   | Switching costs, |
   859→|   |                                   |        |        | portfolio feature|
   860→|   |                                   |        |        | personalization  |
   861→+---+-----------------------------------+--------+--------+------------------+
   862→| 3 | Stripe fee increase              | Low    | Medium | No alternative at|
   863→|   |                                   |        |        | this scale; fees |
   864→|   |                                   |        |        | are small % of   |
   865→|   |                                   |        |        | total cost anyway|
   866→+---+-----------------------------------+--------+--------+------------------+
   867→| 4 | AI API price increase            | Medium | Low    | Pipeline costs   |
   868→|   | (Anthropic, OpenAI)              |        |        | are <$3/mo; even |
   869→|   |                                   |        |        | 5x increase is   |
   870→|   |                                   |        |        | only $15/mo      |
   871→+---+-----------------------------------+--------+--------+------------------+
   872→| 5 | Infrastructure cost spike        | Low    | Low    | Sub-linear       |
   873→|   | (scaling beyond 5K users)        |        |        | scaling; most    |
   874→|   |                                   |        |        | costs are shared |
   875→+---+-----------------------------------+--------+--------+------------------+
   876→| 6 | Enterprise underpricing leaves   | HIGH   | Medium | Price increase   |
   877→|   | revenue on table                 |        |        | planned for Mo 4 |
   878→+---+-----------------------------------+--------+--------+------------------+
   879→| 7 | No expansion revenue mechanism   | HIGH   | Medium | Validation add-on|
   880→|   |                                   |        |        | and API pricing  |
   881→|   |                                   |        |        | not yet live     |
   882→+---+-----------------------------------+--------+--------+------------------+
   883→| 8 | Solo founder capacity constraint | HIGH   | HIGH   | Automation-first |
   884→|   |                                   |        |        | architecture;    |
   885→|   |                                   |        |        | pipeline is fully|
   886→|   |                                   |        |        | automated already|
   887→+---+-----------------------------------+--------+--------+------------------+
   888→```
   889→
   890→### What Could Kill Unit Economics
   891→
   892→1. **Churn > 10%/month**: LTV drops below $190. Still profitable but
   893→   growth becomes a treadmill. Monthly subscribers churn faster than
   894→   annual -- push annual billing aggressively.
   895→
   896→2. **Paid acquisition dependency**: If organic channels dry up after
   897→   Month 3 and growth requires $20+ CAC, payback period extends to
   898→   1-2 months. Still healthy but reduces cash efficiency.
   899→
   900→3. **Feature creep increasing variable costs**: Adding real-time
   901→   scraping, per-user AI generation, or heavy API features would
   902→   shift the cost structure from near-zero marginal cost to
   903→   usage-proportional. Carefully meter any feature that adds
   904→   per-user AI API calls.
   905→
   906→---
   907→
   908→## Appendix: Data Sources
   909→
   910→All financial data derived from:
   911→
   912→- `/src/api/config/stripe.ts` -- Stripe price IDs and amounts
   913→- `/src/api/config/tiers.ts` -- Tier limits and feature access
   914→- `/src/api/services/billing.ts` -- Payment flow implementation
   915→- `/src/config/models.ts` -- AI model pricing per 1M tokens
   916→- `/src/cli/validate-costs.ts` -- Pipeline cost validation targets
   917→- `/src/delivery/email.ts` -- Email sending configuration
   918→- `/src/api/db/schema.ts` -- Database schema and tables
   919→- `/scripts/stripe-setup.ts` -- Stripe product/price creation
   920→- `/Strategy-Analysis.md` -- Market sizing, competitive analysis
   921→- `/Feature-Plan.md` -- Pricing tiers and feature matrix
   922→- `/Context.md` -- Current state and cost profile
   923→
   924→Industry benchmarks based on established SaaS financial benchmarks
   925→(Stripe published rates, SaaS Capital annual survey data, OpenView
   926→expansion benchmarks, Bessemer Cloud Index metrics). Web search for
   927→current 2026 pricing was unavailable during this audit; benchmarks
   928→are drawn from established knowledge of these vendors' pricing
   929→structures through early 2025.
   930→

content.content:
1→# ZeroToShip -- Data Analyst Metrics & Instrumentation Audit
     2→
     3→**Date**: 2026-02-28
     4→**Scope**: Database schema, API routes, pipeline orchestrator, delivery system, frontend pages
     5→**Status**: READ-ONLY audit -- no code changes
     6→
     7→---
     8→
     9→## Table of Contents
    10→
    11→1. [Data Model Summary](#1-data-model-summary)
    12→2. [KPI Framework](#2-kpi-framework)
    13→3. [SQL Queries for Each KPI](#3-sql-queries-for-each-kpi)
    14→4. [Instrumentation Gaps](#4-instrumentation-gaps)
    15→5. [Dashboard Design](#5-dashboard-design)
    16→6. [Cohort Analysis Framework](#6-cohort-analysis-framework)
    17→
    18→---
    19→
    20→## 1. Data Model Summary
    21→
    22→### Core Tables (from `src/api/db/schema.ts` and migrations)
    23→
    24→| Table | Purpose | Key Columns |
    25→|-------|---------|-------------|
    26→| `users` | User accounts (Supabase Auth) | `id`, `email`, `name`, `is_admin`, `created_at` |
    27→| `user_preferences` | Filtering & email prefs | `user_id`, `categories`, `max_effort`, `email_frequency`, `min_priority_score` |
    28→| `subscriptions` | Stripe billing state | `user_id`, `plan`, `status`, `stripe_customer_id`, `current_period_start`, `current_period_end`, `cancel_at_period_end` |
    29→| `ideas` | Generated startup briefs | `id`, `name`, `priority_score`, `effort_estimate`, `category`, `is_published`, `published_at`, `generated_at`, `sources` |
    30→| `saved_ideas` | User bookmarks | `user_id`, `idea_id`, `saved_at` |
    31→| `viewed_ideas` | User view history | `user_id`, `idea_id`, `viewed_at` (unique per user+idea) |
    32→| `spec_generations` | Agent-spec outputs | `user_id`, `idea_id`, `spec`, `created_at` |
    33→| `pipeline_runs` | Scheduler execution log | `run_id`, `status`, `generation_mode`, `started_at`, `completed_at`, `stats`, `api_metrics`, `generation_diagnostics` |
    34→| `email_logs` | Per-email delivery tracking | `run_id`, `user_id`, `recipient_email`, `status`, `message_id`, `sent_at`, `delivered_at`, `opened_at` |
    35→| `onboarding_emails` | Drip campaign tracking | `user_id`, `email_type` (welcome/day1/day3/day7), `sent_at` |
    36→| `usage_tracking` | Daily AI-generation limits | `user_id`, `date`, `fresh_briefs_used`, `validation_requests_used`, `overage_briefs`, `overage_amount_cents` |
    37→| `api_keys` | Enterprise API keys | `user_id`, `key_hash`, `key_prefix`, `last_used_at`, `is_active` |
    38→| `validation_requests` | Enterprise deep-dive requests | `user_id`, `idea_id`, `status`, `result` |
    39→| `webhook_events` | Stripe idempotency | `stripe_event_id`, `event_type`, `processed_at` |
    40→| `rate_limits` | Request throttling | `identifier`, `endpoint`, `request_count`, `window_start`, `window_end` |
    41→
    42→### Entity Relationships
    43→
    44→```
    45→users 1--1 user_preferences
    46→users 1--1 subscriptions
    47→users 1--* saved_ideas *--1 ideas
    48→users 1--* viewed_ideas *--1 ideas
    49→users 1--* spec_generations *--1 ideas
    50→users 1--* email_logs
    51→users 1--* onboarding_emails
    52→users 1--* usage_tracking
    53→users 1--* api_keys
    54→users 1--* validation_requests *--1 ideas
    55→pipeline_runs (standalone -- linked to email_logs via run_id text FK)
    56→```
    57→
    58→---
    59→
    60→## 2. KPI Framework
    61→
    62→### North Star Metric
    63→
    64→**Weekly Active Engaged Users (WAEU)** -- users who viewed at least 1 idea detail page OR saved/bookmarked at least 1 idea OR generated at least 1 spec in the trailing 7 days.
    65→
    66→Rationale: ZeroToShip is a "social signal scraper for identifying emerging startup ideas." Value is delivered when users actively consume and act on the ideas. Views, saves, and spec generations are the clearest signals that a user is extracting value from the product.
    67→
    68→---
    69→
    70→### Primary Metrics (Health Indicators)
    71→
    72→| # | Metric | Definition | Why It Matters |
    73→|---|--------|------------|----------------|
    74→| P1 | **Subscriber MRR** | `SUM(plan_price)` for all `subscriptions` with `status='active'` | Revenue health -- the business survives on this |
    75→| P2 | **Daily Active Users (DAU)** | Distinct users with a `viewed_ideas` or `saved_ideas` or `spec_generations` event today | Daily engagement pulse |
    76→| P3 | **Pipeline Success Rate** | % of `pipeline_runs` with `success=true` in trailing 7 days | Content production reliability |
    77→| P4 | **Email Open Rate** | % of `email_logs` where `opened_at IS NOT NULL` among those with `status IN ('sent','delivered')` | Delivery channel health |
    78→| P5 | **Free-to-Pro Conversion Rate** | Users upgrading from `plan='free'` to `plan='pro'` / total free users, trailing 30 days | Monetization efficiency |
    79→
    80→---
    81→
    82→### Secondary Metrics (Diagnostic)
    83→
    84→| # | Metric | Definition | Diagnoses |
    85→|---|--------|------------|-----------|
    86→| S1 | Ideas generated per run | `stats->>'ideasGenerated'` from `pipeline_runs` | Content volume adequacy |
    87→| S2 | Brief quality pass rate | `generation_diagnostics->>'qualityPassRate'` from `pipeline_runs` | AI generation quality |
    88→| S3 | Save rate | `COUNT(saved_ideas)` / `COUNT(viewed_ideas)` per day | Content-market fit signal |
    89→| S4 | Spec generation per user per month | `COUNT(spec_generations)` grouped by user, month | Feature adoption depth |
    90→| S5 | Email bounce/complaint rate | `email_logs` where `status IN ('bounced','complained')` / total sent | List hygiene |
    91→| S6 | Onboarding drip completion | % of users who received all 4 emails (welcome, day1, day3, day7) | Onboarding funnel health |
    92→| S7 | Churn rate | Users whose subscriptions transition to `cancel_at_period_end=true` or `status != 'active'` | Retention risk |
    93→| S8 | Pipeline cost per run | `api_metrics->>'estimatedCost'` from `pipeline_runs` | Unit economics |
    94→| S9 | Avg priority score of published ideas | `AVG(priority_score)` on `ideas` where `is_published=true` | Content quality baseline |
    95→| S10 | API key utilization (Enterprise) | `api_keys` with `last_used_at` in trailing 7 days / total active keys | Enterprise engagement |
    96→
    97→---
    98→
    99→### Leading Indicators (Predictive)
   100→
   101→| # | Metric | What It Predicts | Signal |
   102→|---|--------|-----------------|--------|
   103→| L1 | Day-1 retention | Long-term retention | Users who view an idea within 24h of signup |
   104→| L2 | Onboarding email open rate | Activation probability | Users who open the welcome email |
   105→| L3 | Saved ideas in first 7 days | Conversion likelihood | Users who save >= 1 idea in their first week |
   106→| L4 | Spec gen in first 14 days | Pro conversion | Users who generate a spec early tend to upgrade |
   107→| L5 | Pipeline error frequency increase | Pipeline stability regression | Rising error count across last 5 runs |
   108→| L6 | Cancel-at-period-end flag set | Imminent churn | Subscriptions with `cancel_at_period_end=true` and active |
   109→
   110→---
   111→
   112→## 3. SQL Queries for Each KPI
   113→
   114→### North Star: Weekly Active Engaged Users (WAEU)
   115→
   116→```sql
   117→-- WAEU: users who viewed, saved, or generated a spec in the trailing 7 days
   118→SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS waeu
   119→FROM (
   120→    SELECT user_id FROM viewed_ideas
   121→    WHERE viewed_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days'
   122→    UNION
   123→    SELECT user_id FROM saved_ideas
   124→    WHERE saved_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days'
   125→    UNION
   126→    SELECT user_id FROM spec_generations
   127→    WHERE created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days'
   128→) AS engaged_users;
   129→```
   130→
   131→### P1: Subscriber MRR
   132→
   133→```sql
   134→-- MRR by plan (requires mapping plan to price; shown as plan distribution)
   135→SELECT
   136→    s.plan,
   137→    COUNT(*) AS active_count,
   138→    CASE s.plan
   139→        WHEN 'pro' THEN COUNT(*) * 19  -- $19/mo
   140→        WHEN 'enterprise' THEN COUNT(*) * 99  -- $99/mo assumption
   141→        ELSE 0
   142→    END AS estimated_mrr_usd
   143→FROM subscriptions s
   144→WHERE s.status = 'active'
   145→  AND s.plan != 'free'
   146→GROUP BY s.plan;
   147→```
   148→
   149→### P2: Daily Active Users (DAU)
   150→
   151→```sql
   152→-- DAU: distinct users with engagement today
   153→SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS dau
   154→FROM (
   155→    SELECT user_id FROM viewed_ideas
   156→    WHERE viewed_at::date = CURRENT_DATE
   157→    UNION
   158→    SELECT user_id FROM saved_ideas
   159→    WHERE saved_at::date = CURRENT_DATE
   160→    UNION
   161→    SELECT user_id FROM spec_generations
   162→    WHERE created_at::date = CURRENT_DATE
   163→) AS active_today;
   164→```
   165→
   166→### P2b: DAU Trend (trailing 30 days)
   167→
   168→```sql
   169→-- DAU trend: one row per day for the last 30 days
   170→SELECT
   171→    d.day,
   172→    COUNT(DISTINCT e.user_id) AS dau
   173→FROM generate_series(
   174→    CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days',
   175→    CURRENT_DATE,
   176→    '1 day'::interval
   177→) AS d(day)
   178→LEFT JOIN (
   179→    SELECT user_id, viewed_at::date AS activity_date FROM viewed_ideas
   180→    UNION ALL
   181→    SELECT user_id, saved_at::date FROM saved_ideas
   182→    UNION ALL
   183→    SELECT user_id, created_at::date FROM spec_generations
   184→) AS e ON e.activity_date = d.day
   185→GROUP BY d.day
   186→ORDER BY d.day;
   187→```
   188→
   189→### P3: Pipeline Success Rate (trailing 7 days)
   190→
   191→```sql
   192→SELECT
   193→    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE success = true) AS successful_runs,
   194→    COUNT(*) AS total_runs,
   195→    ROUND(
   196→        100.0 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE success = true) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0),
   197→        1
   198→    ) AS success_rate_pct
   199→FROM pipeline_runs
   200→WHERE started_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days';
   201→```
   202→
   203→### P4: Email Open Rate
   204→
   205→```sql
   206→-- Open rate among delivered/sent emails in the last 30 days
   207→SELECT
   208→    COUNT(*) AS total_sent,
   209→    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE opened_at IS NOT NULL) AS opened,
   210→    ROUND(
   211→        100.0 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE opened_at IS NOT NULL)
   212→        / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0),
   213→        1
   214→    ) AS open_rate_pct,
   215→    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE status = 'delivered') AS delivered,
   216→    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE status = 'bounced') AS bounced,
   217→    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE status = 'complained') AS complained
   218→FROM email_logs
   219→WHERE sent_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '30 days'
   220→  AND status IN ('sent', 'delivered');
   221→```
   222→
   223→### P5: Free-to-Pro Conversion Rate (trailing 30 days)
   224→
   225→```sql
   226→-- Users who upgraded in the last 30 days as a % of total free users at period start
   227→WITH upgrades AS (
   228→    SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT s.user_id) AS upgraded_count
   229→    FROM subscriptions s
   230→    WHERE s.plan = 'pro'
   231→      AND s.status = 'active'
   232→      AND s.updated_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '30 days'
   233→      -- Only count users whose row was created before the window (existing free users)
   234→      AND s.created_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '30 days'
   235→),
   236→free_base AS (
   237→    SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT u.id) AS free_users
   238→    FROM users u
   239→    LEFT JOIN subscriptions s ON s.user_id = u.id
   240→    WHERE COALESCE(s.plan, 'free') = 'free'
   241→      AND u.created_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '30 days'
   242→)
   243→SELECT
   244→    f.free_users,
   245→    u.upgraded_count,
   246→    ROUND(100.0 * u.upgraded_count / NULLIF(f.free_users, 0), 2) AS conversion_rate_pct
   247→FROM free_base f, upgrades u;
   248→```
   249→
   250→### S1: Ideas Generated Per Run
   251→
   252→```sql
   253→SELECT
   254→    run_id,
   255→    started_at,
   256→    (stats->>'ideasGenerated')::int AS ideas_generated,
   257→    (stats->>'postsScraped')::int AS posts_scraped,
   258→    (stats->>'clustersCreated')::int AS clusters_created,
   259→    (stats->>'emailsSent')::int AS emails_sent,
   260→    success,
   261→    total_duration
   262→FROM pipeline_runs
   263→ORDER BY started_at DESC
   264→LIMIT 20;
   265→```
   266→
   267→### S2: Brief Quality Pass Rate Trend
   268→
   269→```sql
   270→SELECT
   271→    run_id,
   272→    started_at,
   273→    generation_mode,
   274→    (generation_diagnostics->>'qualityPassRate')::float AS quality_pass_rate,
   275→    (generation_diagnostics->>'fallbackRate')::float AS fallback_rate,
   276→    (generation_diagnostics->>'costPerBriefUsd')::float AS cost_per_brief_usd,
   277→    (generation_diagnostics->>'latencyPerBriefMs')::int AS latency_per_brief_ms
   278→FROM pipeline_runs
   279→WHERE generation_diagnostics IS NOT NULL
   280→ORDER BY started_at DESC
   281→LIMIT 20;
   282→```
   283→
   284→### S3: Save Rate (views to saves ratio)
   285→
   286→```sql
   287→-- Daily save rate: bookmarks / views
   288→SELECT
   289→    d.day,
   290→    COALESCE(v.view_count, 0) AS views,
   291→    COALESCE(s.save_count, 0) AS saves,
   292→    CASE WHEN COALESCE(v.view_count, 0) > 0
   293→        THEN ROUND(100.0 * COALESCE(s.save_count, 0) / v.view_count, 1)
   294→        ELSE 0
   295→    END AS save_rate_pct
   296→FROM generate_series(
   297→    CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days',
   298→    CURRENT_DATE,
   299→    '1 day'::interval
   300→) AS d(day)
   301→LEFT JOIN (
   302→    SELECT viewed_at::date AS day, COUNT(*) AS view_count
   303→    FROM viewed_ideas
   304→    GROUP BY viewed_at::date
   305→) v ON v.day = d.day
   306→LEFT JOIN (
   307→    SELECT saved_at::date AS day, COUNT(*) AS save_count
   308→    FROM saved_ideas
   309→    GROUP BY saved_at::date
   310→) s ON s.day = d.day
   311→ORDER BY d.day;
   312→```
   313→
   314→### S4: Spec Generations Per User Per Month
   315→
   316→```sql
   317→SELECT
   318→    DATE_TRUNC('month', sg.created_at) AS month,
   319→    sg.user_id,
   320→    u.email,
   321→    COALESCE(s.plan, 'free') AS plan,
   322→    COUNT(*) AS spec_count
   323→FROM spec_generations sg
   324→JOIN users u ON u.id = sg.user_id
   325→LEFT JOIN subscriptions s ON s.user_id = sg.user_id
   326→GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('month', sg.created_at), sg.user_id, u.email, s.plan
   327→ORDER BY month DESC, spec_count DESC;
   328→```
   329→
   330→### S5: Email Bounce/Complaint Rate
   331→
   332→```sql
   333→SELECT
   334→    DATE_TRUNC('week', sent_at) AS week,
   335→    COUNT(*) AS total,
   336→    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE status = 'bounced') AS bounced,
   337→    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE status = 'complained') AS complained,
   338→    ROUND(
   339→        100.0 * (
   340→            COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE status IN ('bounced', 'complained'))
   341→        ) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0),
   342→        2
   343→    ) AS bounce_complaint_rate_pct
   344→FROM email_logs
   345→GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('week', sent_at)
   346→ORDER BY week DESC
   347→LIMIT 12;
   348→```
   349→
   350→### S6: Onboarding Drip Completion Funnel
   351→
   352→```sql
   353→-- Per-email-type completion rates
   354→WITH user_base AS (
   355→    SELECT id, created_at
   356→    FROM users
   357→    WHERE created_at <= NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days'  -- only users old enough to receive all emails
   358→)
   359→SELECT
   360→    'welcome' AS email_type,
   361→    COUNT(ub.id) AS eligible_users,
   362→    COUNT(oe.id) AS received,
   363→    ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(oe.id) / NULLIF(COUNT(ub.id), 0), 1) AS completion_pct
   364→FROM user_base ub
   365→LEFT JOIN onboarding_emails oe
   366→    ON oe.user_id = ub.id AND oe.email_type = 'welcome'
   367→
   368→UNION ALL
   369→
   370→SELECT 'day1', COUNT(ub.id), COUNT(oe.id),
   371→    ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(oe.id) / NULLIF(COUNT(ub.id), 0), 1)
   372→FROM user_base ub
   373→LEFT JOIN onboarding_emails oe ON oe.user_id = ub.id AND oe.email_type = 'day1'
   374→
   375→UNION ALL
   376→
   377→SELECT 'day3', COUNT(ub.id), COUNT(oe.id),
   378→    ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(oe.id) / NULLIF(COUNT(ub.id), 0), 1)
   379→FROM user_base ub
   380→LEFT JOIN onboarding_emails oe ON oe.user_id = ub.id AND oe.email_type = 'day3'
   381→
   382→UNION ALL
   383→
   384→SELECT 'day7', COUNT(ub.id), COUNT(oe.id),
   385→    ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(oe.id) / NULLIF(COUNT(ub.id), 0), 1)
   386→FROM user_base ub
   387→LEFT JOIN onboarding_emails oe ON oe.user_id = ub.id AND oe.email_type = 'day7'
   388→
   389→ORDER BY
   390→    CASE email_type
   391→        WHEN 'welcome' THEN 1
   392→        WHEN 'day1' THEN 2
   393→        WHEN 'day3' THEN 3
   394→        WHEN 'day7' THEN 4
   395→    END;
   396→```
   397→
   398→### S7: Churn Rate (monthly)
   399→
   400→```sql
   401→-- Users who cancelled or lapsed in the last 30 days
   402→SELECT
   403→    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE cancel_at_period_end = true AND status = 'active') AS pending_cancellation,
   404→    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE status IN ('canceled', 'cancelled', 'past_due')) AS churned,
   405→    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE status = 'active') AS active,
   406→    ROUND(
   407→        100.0 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE status IN ('canceled', 'cancelled', 'past_due'))
   408→        / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0),
   409→        1
   410→    ) AS churn_rate_pct
   411→FROM subscriptions
   412→WHERE plan != 'free';
   413→```
   414→
   415→### S8: Pipeline Cost Per Run Trend
   416→
   417→```sql
   418→SELECT
   419→    run_id,
   420→    started_at,
   421→    (api_metrics->>'estimatedCost')::float AS estimated_cost_usd,
   422→    (api_metrics->>'totalCalls')::int AS total_api_calls,
   423→    (api_metrics->>'totalInputTokens')::int AS input_tokens,
   424→    (api_metrics->>'totalOutputTokens')::int AS output_tokens,
   425→    (stats->>'ideasGenerated')::int AS ideas_generated,
   426→    CASE WHEN (stats->>'ideasGenerated')::int > 0
   427→        THEN ROUND(
   428→            (api_metrics->>'estimatedCost')::numeric
   429→            / (stats->>'ideasGenerated')::int,
   430→            4
   431→        )
   432→        ELSE NULL
   433→    END AS cost_per_idea_usd
   434→FROM pipeline_runs
   435→WHERE api_metrics IS NOT NULL
   436→ORDER BY started_at DESC
   437→LIMIT 20;
   438→```
   439→
   440→### S9: Average Priority Score of Published Ideas (weekly)
   441→
   442→```sql
   443→SELECT
   444→    DATE_TRUNC('week', published_at) AS week,
   445→    COUNT(*) AS published_count,
   446→    ROUND(AVG(priority_score::numeric), 2) AS avg_priority_score,
   447→    ROUND(MIN(priority_score::numeric), 2) AS min_priority,
   448→    ROUND(MAX(priority_score::numeric), 2) AS max_priority
   449→FROM ideas
   450→WHERE is_published = true
   451→  AND published_at IS NOT NULL
   452→GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('week', published_at)
   453→ORDER BY week DESC
   454→LIMIT 12;
   455→```
   456→
   457→### L1: Day-1 Retention Rate
   458→
   459→```sql
   460→-- % of users who viewed an idea within 24h of signup
   461→WITH signups AS (
   462→    SELECT id, created_at
   463→    FROM users
   464→    WHERE created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '30 days'
   465→),
   466→d1_active AS (
   467→    SELECT DISTINCT s.id
   468→    FROM signups s
   469→    JOIN viewed_ideas vi ON vi.user_id = s.id
   470→    WHERE vi.viewed_at <= s.created_at + INTERVAL '24 hours'
   471→)
   472→SELECT
   473→    COUNT(s.id) AS total_signups,
   474→    COUNT(d.id) AS day1_active,
   475→    ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(d.id) / NULLIF(COUNT(s.id), 0), 1) AS day1_retention_pct
   476→FROM signups s
   477→LEFT JOIN d1_active d ON d.id = s.id;
   478→```
   479→
   480→### L2: Onboarding Welcome Email Open Rate
   481→
   482→```sql
   483→-- Open rate for welcome emails specifically
   484→SELECT
   485→    COUNT(*) AS total_welcome_sent,
   486→    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE el.opened_at IS NOT NULL) AS opened,
   487→    ROUND(
   488→        100.0 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE el.opened_at IS NOT NULL)
   489→        / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0),
   490→        1
   491→    ) AS welcome_open_rate_pct
   492→FROM onboarding_emails oe
   493→JOIN email_logs el ON el.user_id = oe.user_id
   494→    AND el.subject LIKE 'Welcome to ZeroToShip%'
   495→WHERE oe.email_type = 'welcome';
   496→```
   497→
   498→### L3: Saved Ideas in First 7 Days
   499→
   500→```sql
   501→-- Users who saved at least 1 idea in their first week
   502→WITH new_users AS (
   503→    SELECT id, created_at
   504→    FROM users
   505→    WHERE created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '60 days'
   506→      AND created_at <= NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days'
   507→),
   508→savers AS (
   509→    SELECT DISTINCT nu.id
   510→    FROM new_users nu
   511→    JOIN saved_ideas si ON si.user_id = nu.id
   512→    WHERE si.saved_at <= nu.created_at + INTERVAL '7 days'
   513→)
   514→SELECT
   515→    COUNT(nu.id) AS total_new_users,
   516→    COUNT(s.id) AS saved_in_first_week,
   517→    ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(s.id) / NULLIF(COUNT(nu.id), 0), 1) AS first_week_save_rate_pct
   518→FROM new_users nu
   519→LEFT JOIN savers s ON s.id = nu.id;
   520→```
   521→
   522→### L5: Pipeline Error Frequency (last 10 runs)
   523→
   524→```sql
   525→SELECT
   526→    run_id,
   527→    started_at,
   528→    success,
   529→    status,
   530→    jsonb_array_length(COALESCE(errors, '[]'::jsonb)) AS error_count,
   531→    last_completed_phase,
   532→    total_duration
   533→FROM pipeline_runs
   534→ORDER BY started_at DESC
   535→LIMIT 10;
   536→```
   537→
   538→### L6: Pending Cancellations
   539→
   540→```sql
   541→SELECT
   542→    u.id,
   543→    u.email,
   544→    s.plan,
   545→    s.current_period_end,
   546→    s.updated_at
   547→FROM subscriptions s
   548→JOIN users u ON u.id = s.user_id
   549→WHERE s.cancel_at_period_end = true
   550→  AND s.status = 'active'
   551→ORDER BY s.current_period_end ASC;
   552→```
   553→
   554→---
   555→
   556→## 4. Instrumentation Gaps
   557→
   558→### Critical Gaps (High Priority)
   559→
   560→| # | Gap | Impact | Recommended Fix |
   561→|---|-----|--------|-----------------|
   562→| G1 | **No page view tracking** | Cannot measure landing page performance, explore page views, or funnel conversion | Add a `page_views` table or integrate a lightweight analytics service (e.g., Plausible, PostHog). Schema: `(id, user_id nullable, session_id, path, referrer, utm_source, utm_medium, utm_campaign, created_at)` |
   563→| G2 | **No signup source attribution** | Cannot determine which channels drive signups (organic, email, referral, ad) | Add `signup_source`, `utm_source`, `utm_medium`, `utm_campaign` columns to `users` table |
   564→| G3 | **No email click tracking** | Have open tracking but no link-click tracking; cannot measure CTR from daily digest to idea detail pages | Add `clicked_at` column to `email_logs` or create an `email_clicks` table with link URL |
   565→| G4 | **No idea detail view duration** | `viewed_ideas` is a unique constraint (1 row per user+idea), so repeat views and dwell time are not captured | Change `viewed_ideas` to non-unique or add a separate `idea_impressions` table with `(user_id, idea_id, viewed_at, duration_seconds)` |
   566→| G5 | **Enterprise usage stats are hardcoded zeros** | The `/api/v1/stats` endpoint returns `{ ideasViewed: 0, searchQueries: 0, ... }` with a `// TODO` comment | Query actual counts from `viewed_ideas`, `validation_requests`, and `rate_limits` for the authenticated enterprise user |
   567→
   568→### Important Gaps (Medium Priority)
   569→
   570→| # | Gap | Impact | Recommended Fix |
   571→|---|-----|--------|-----------------|
   572→| G6 | **No search query logging** | Cannot analyze what users search for, identify content gaps, or measure search result quality | Create a `search_queries` table: `(id, user_id, query_text, result_count, clicked_result_id nullable, created_at)` |
   573→| G7 | **No frontend event tracking** | No button click tracking, no scroll depth, no feature discovery metrics | Integrate a product analytics SDK (PostHog, Amplitude, or Mixpanel) on the Next.js frontend |
   574→| G8 | **No time-to-first-value (TTFV) metric** | Cannot measure how quickly new users reach their "aha moment" | Define TTFV as "time from signup to first idea view" and compute from `users.created_at` vs first `viewed_ideas.viewed_at` |
   575→| G9 | **No subscription lifecycle events table** | Cannot analyze upgrade/downgrade/cancel patterns over time; `subscriptions` only stores current state | Create a `subscription_events` table: `(id, user_id, event_type, from_plan, to_plan, created_at)` |
   576→| G10 | **No per-idea engagement aggregates** | Calculating save counts and view counts per idea requires full-table scans on junction tables | Add materialized view or denormalized columns: `ideas.view_count`, `ideas.save_count` |
   577→| G11 | **Onboarding emails not in email_logs** | Onboarding emails (welcome, day1, day3, day7) are tracked in `onboarding_emails` but not in `email_logs`, so they have no delivery/open tracking | Write onboarding email sends to `email_logs` as well, or merge the tables |
   578→| G12 | **No daily digest email-to-idea linking** | `email_logs` tracks that a digest was sent, but not which ideas were included; cannot measure per-idea click-through from email | Add `idea_ids` JSONB column to `email_logs` or create a junction table |
   579→
   580→### Nice-to-Have Gaps (Lower Priority)
   581→
   582→| # | Gap | Recommended Fix |
   583→|---|-----|-----------------|
   584→| G13 | **No API endpoint latency tracking** | Add request duration logging via Fastify hooks and persist to a `request_logs` table or external monitoring |
   585→| G14 | **No A/B test framework** | Add a `feature_flags` / `experiments` table for controlled rollouts |
   586→| G15 | **No NPS/CSAT collection** | The day7 onboarding email asks for feedback via email reply; formalize with an in-app survey and track scores |
   587→| G16 | **No scraper source quality tracking** | Track per-source (Reddit, HN, Twitter, GitHub) signal quality over time to optimize scraper weighting |
   588→
   589→---
   590→
   591→## 5. Dashboard Design
   592→
   593→### 5.1 Executive Dashboard (refreshes every 15 minutes)
   594→
   595→**Layout**: Single page, 4 sections stacked vertically.
   596→
   597→```
   598→+---------------------------------------------------------------+
   599→|  NORTH STAR: WAEU                                              |
   600→|  [Large number]   [Sparkline trailing 12 weeks]                |
   601→+---------------------------------------------------------------+
   602→
   603→+-----------------+-----------------+-----------------+-----------+
   604→| MRR             | DAU             | Pipeline Status | Signups   |
   605→| $X,XXX          | XX              | [green/red]     | X today   |
   606→| [trend arrow]   | [trend arrow]   | Last run: HH:MM | [7d trend]|
   607→+-----------------+-----------------+-----------------+-----------+
   608→
   609→+-------------------------------+-------------------------------+
   610→| Email Health (30 days)        | Conversion Funnel             |
   611→| - Sent: X,XXX                | Signup --> View --> Save --> Spec --> Upgrade
   612→| - Open rate: XX%             | [horizontal funnel bars]       |
   613→| - Bounce rate: X.X%          |                               |
   614→| - Complaint rate: X.X%       |                               |
   615→+-------------------------------+-------------------------------+
   616→
   617→+-------------------------------+-------------------------------+
   618→| Revenue by Plan               | Churn Watch                  |
   619→| [Stacked bar: free/pro/ent]   | Pending cancel: X            |
   620→| [Monthly trend line]          | Churned this month: X        |
   621→|                               | At-risk (no activity 14d): X |
   622→+-------------------------------+-------------------------------+
   623→```
   624→
   625→### 5.2 Pipeline Operations Dashboard (refreshes every 5 minutes)
   626→
   627→```
   628→+---------------------------------------------------------------+
   629→| Pipeline Health                                                |
   630→| Success rate (7d): XX%  |  Avg duration: XXs  |  Next run: HH:MM |
   631→+---------------------------------------------------------------+
   632→
   633→+-------------------------------+-------------------------------+
   634→| Last 10 Runs                  | Cost Trend                   |
   635→| [Table: runId, status, time,  | [Line chart: cost per run]   |
   636→|  ideas generated, duration,   | [Line chart: cost per idea]  |
   637→|  emails sent, errors]         |                               |
   638→+-------------------------------+-------------------------------+
   639→
   640→+-------------------------------+-------------------------------+
   641→| Quality Metrics               | Scraper Source Distribution   |
   642→| Quality pass rate: XX%       | Reddit: XX posts              |
   643→| Fallback rate: XX%           | HN: XX posts                  |
   644→| Avg priority score: XX       | Twitter: XX posts             |
   645→|                               | GitHub: XX posts              |
   646→+-------------------------------+-------------------------------+
   647→
   648→+---------------------------------------------------------------+
   649→| Generation Diagnostics (Latest Run)                           |
   650→| Fallback reasons: [bar chart by reason code]                  |
   651→| Quality failures: [bar chart by failure code]                 |
   652→| Budget stop: [yes/no with details]                            |
   653→| Publish gate: [auto-published X, needs review X]             |
   654→+---------------------------------------------------------------+
   655→```
   656→
   657→### 5.3 User Engagement Dashboard (refreshes hourly)
   658→
   659→```
   660→+-------------------------------+-------------------------------+
   661→| DAU Trend (30 days)           | Retention Curve              |
   662→| [Line chart]                  | [Day 1/7/14/30 retention %]  |
   663→+-------------------------------+-------------------------------+
   664→
   665→+-------------------------------+-------------------------------+
   666→| Onboarding Funnel             | Feature Adoption             |
   667→| Welcome: XX%                  | Idea views: XX%              |
   668→| Day 1: XX%                   | Saves: XX%                   |
   669→| Day 3: XX%                   | Spec gen: XX%                |
   670→| Day 7: XX%                   | Preferences set: XX%         |
   671→+-------------------------------+-------------------------------+
   672→
   673→+---------------------------------------------------------------+
   674→| Top Ideas This Week                                           |
   675→| [Table: idea name, views, saves, specs generated, category]   |
   676→+---------------------------------------------------------------+
   677→
   678→+-------------------------------+-------------------------------+
   679→| Save Rate Trend               | Spec Gen Volume              |
   680→| [Line chart: daily save rate] | [Bar chart: daily spec gens] |
   681→+-------------------------------+-------------------------------+
   682→```
   683→
   684→### 5.4 Email Performance Dashboard (refreshes every 30 minutes)
   685→
   686→```
   687→+-------------------------------+-------------------------------+
   688→| Delivery Funnel (30 days)     | Open Rate Trend              |
   689→| Sent --> Delivered --> Opened  | [Line chart: weekly]         |
   690→| X,XXX --> X,XXX --> X,XXX     |                              |
   691→+-------------------------------+-------------------------------+
   692→
   693→+-------------------------------+-------------------------------+
   694→| Bounce/Complaint Monitor      | Per-Run Delivery             |
   695→| [Line chart: weekly rates]    | [Table: runId, sent, failed, |
   696→| ALERT if > 2% bounce rate    |  delivered, opened]           |
   697→+-------------------------------+-------------------------------+
   698→```
   699→
   700→---
   701→
   702→## 6. Cohort Analysis Framework
   703→
   704→### 6.1 Signup Cohort (Weekly)
   705→
   706→The foundational cohort: group users by the week they signed up and track their behavior over time.
   707→
   708→```sql
   709→-- Weekly signup cohorts with retention at day 1, 7, 14, 30
   710→WITH cohorts AS (
   711→    SELECT
   712→        id AS user_id,
   713→        DATE_TRUNC('week', created_at)::date AS cohort_week,
   714→        created_at
   715→    FROM users
   716→    WHERE created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '90 days'
   717→),
   718→activity AS (
   719→    SELECT user_id, viewed_at AS activity_at FROM viewed_ideas
   720→    UNION ALL
   721→    SELECT user_id, saved_at FROM saved_ideas
   722→    UNION ALL
   723→    SELECT user_id, created_at FROM spec_generations
   724→)
   725→SELECT
   726→    c.cohort_week,
   727→    COUNT(DISTINCT c.user_id) AS cohort_size,
   728→    COUNT(DISTINCT CASE
   729→        WHEN a.activity_at BETWEEN c.created_at AND c.created_at + INTERVAL '1 day'
   730→        THEN c.user_id
   731→    END) AS active_day1,
   732→    COUNT(DISTINCT CASE
   733→        WHEN a.activity_at BETWEEN c.created_at + INTERVAL '6 days'
   734→            AND c.created_at + INTERVAL '8 days'
   735→        THEN c.user_id
   736→    END) AS active_week1,
   737→    COUNT(DISTINCT CASE
   738→        WHEN a.activity_at BETWEEN c.created_at + INTERVAL '13 days'
   739→            AND c.created_at + INTERVAL '15 days'
   740→        THEN c.user_id
   741→    END) AS active_week2,
   742→    COUNT(DISTINCT CASE
   743→        WHEN a.activity_at BETWEEN c.created_at + INTERVAL '27 days'
   744→            AND c.created_at + INTERVAL '31 days'
   745→        THEN c.user_id
   746→    END) AS active_month1
   747→FROM cohorts c
   748→LEFT JOIN activity a ON a.user_id = c.user_id
   749→GROUP BY c.cohort_week
   750→ORDER BY c.cohort_week;
   751→```
   752→
   753→### 6.2 Plan Cohort
   754→
   755→Compare behavior of free, pro, and enterprise users.
   756→
   757→```sql
   758→-- Engagement metrics by subscription plan
   759→SELECT
   760→    COALESCE(s.plan, 'free') AS plan,
   761→    COUNT(DISTINCT u.id) AS user_count,
   762→    ROUND(AVG(view_counts.view_count), 1) AS avg_views_per_user,
   763→    ROUND(AVG(save_counts.save_count), 1) AS avg_saves_per_user,
   764→    ROUND(AVG(spec_counts.spec_count), 1) AS avg_specs_per_user
   765→FROM users u
   766→LEFT JOIN subscriptions s ON s.user_id = u.id AND s.status = 'active'
   767→LEFT JOIN (
   768→    SELECT user_id, COUNT(*) AS view_count
   769→    FROM viewed_ideas
   770→    WHERE viewed_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '30 days'
   771→    GROUP BY user_id
   772→) view_counts ON view_counts.user_id = u.id
   773→LEFT JOIN (
   774→    SELECT user_id, COUNT(*) AS save_count
   775→    FROM saved_ideas
   776→    WHERE saved_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '30 days'
   777→    GROUP BY user_id
   778→) save_counts ON save_counts.user_id = u.id
   779→LEFT JOIN (
   780→    SELECT user_id, COUNT(*) AS spec_count
   781→    FROM spec_generations
   782→    WHERE created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '30 days'
   783→    GROUP BY user_id
   784→) spec_counts ON spec_counts.user_id = u.id
   785→GROUP BY COALESCE(s.plan, 'free')
   786→ORDER BY user_count DESC;
   787→```
   788→
   789→### 6.3 Activation Cohort
   790→
   791→Segment users by whether they completed key activation milestones.
   792→
   793→```sql
   794→-- Activation funnel: signup -> view idea -> save idea -> generate spec -> upgrade
   795→WITH user_milestones AS (
   796→    SELECT
   797→        u.id AS user_id,
   798→        u.created_at AS signup_at,
   799→        MIN(vi.viewed_at) AS first_view_at,
   800→        MIN(si.saved_at) AS first_save_at,
   801→        MIN(sg.created_at) AS first_spec_at,
   802→        CASE WHEN s.plan IN ('pro', 'enterprise') THEN s.updated_at ELSE NULL END AS upgrade_at
   803→    FROM users u
   804→    LEFT JOIN viewed_ideas vi ON vi.user_id = u.id
   805→    LEFT JOIN saved_ideas si ON si.user_id = u.id
   806→    LEFT JOIN spec_generations sg ON sg.user_id = u.id
   807→    LEFT JOIN subscriptions s ON s.user_id = u.id AND s.status = 'active'
   808→    WHERE u.created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '90 days'
   809→    GROUP BY u.id, u.created_at, s.plan, s.updated_at
   810→)
   811→SELECT
   812→    COUNT(*) AS total_signups,
   813→    COUNT(first_view_at) AS viewed_idea,
   814→    COUNT(first_save_at) AS saved_idea,
   815→    COUNT(first_spec_at) AS generated_spec,
   816→    COUNT(upgrade_at) AS upgraded,
   817→    ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(first_view_at) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1) AS view_rate_pct,
   818→    ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(first_save_at) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1) AS save_rate_pct,
   819→    ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(first_spec_at) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1) AS spec_rate_pct,
   820→    ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(upgrade_at) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1) AS upgrade_rate_pct
   821→FROM user_milestones;
   822→```
   823→
   824→### 6.4 Email Engagement Cohort
   825→
   826→Compare users who open emails vs those who don't.
   827→
   828→```sql
   829→-- Users who opened at least one email vs those who didn't, and their engagement
   830→WITH email_engagement AS (
   831→    SELECT
   832→        user_id,
   833→        BOOL_OR(opened_at IS NOT NULL) AS has_opened_email
   834→    FROM email_logs
   835→    WHERE sent_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '30 days'
   836→    GROUP BY user_id
   837→)
   838→SELECT
   839→    ee.has_opened_email,
   840→    COUNT(DISTINCT ee.user_id) AS user_count,
   841→    ROUND(AVG(vc.view_count), 1) AS avg_ideas_viewed,
   842→    ROUND(AVG(sc.save_count), 1) AS avg_ideas_saved,
   843→    COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN s.plan IN ('pro', 'enterprise') THEN ee.user_id END) AS paid_users
   844→FROM email_engagement ee
   845→LEFT JOIN (
   846→    SELECT user_id, COUNT(*) AS view_count
   847→    FROM viewed_ideas
   848→    WHERE viewed_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '30 days'
   849→    GROUP BY user_id
   850→) vc ON vc.user_id = ee.user_id
   851→LEFT JOIN (
   852→    SELECT user_id, COUNT(*) AS save_count
   853→    FROM saved_ideas
   854→    WHERE saved_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '30 days'
   855→    GROUP BY user_id
   856→) sc ON sc.user_id = ee.user_id
   857→LEFT JOIN subscriptions s ON s.user_id = ee.user_id AND s.status = 'active'
   858→GROUP BY ee.has_opened_email;
   859→```
   860→
   861→### 6.5 Category/Effort Preference Cohort
   862→
   863→Analyze which user preference segments show highest engagement and conversion.
   864→
   865→```sql
   866→-- User engagement by effort preference
   867→SELECT
   868→    COALESCE(up.max_effort, 'quarter') AS effort_preference,
   869→    COUNT(DISTINCT u.id) AS user_count,
   870→    ROUND(AVG(vc.view_count), 1) AS avg_views,
   871→    ROUND(AVG(sc.save_count), 1) AS avg_saves,
   872→    COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN s.plan IN ('pro', 'enterprise') THEN u.id END) AS paid_users,
   873→    ROUND(
   874→        100.0 * COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN s.plan IN ('pro', 'enterprise') THEN u.id END)
   875→        / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT u.id), 0),
   876→        1
   877→    ) AS paid_conversion_pct
   878→FROM users u
   879→LEFT JOIN user_preferences up ON up.user_id = u.id
   880→LEFT JOIN subscriptions s ON s.user_id = u.id AND s.status = 'active'
   881→LEFT JOIN (
   882→    SELECT user_id, COUNT(*) AS view_count
   883→    FROM viewed_ideas GROUP BY user_id
   884→) vc ON vc.user_id = u.id
   885→LEFT JOIN (
   886→    SELECT user_id, COUNT(*) AS save_count
   887→    FROM saved_ideas GROUP BY user_id
   888→) sc ON sc.user_id = u.id
   889→GROUP BY COALESCE(up.max_effort, 'quarter')
   890→ORDER BY user_count DESC;
   891→```
   892→
   893→### 6.6 Time-to-Value Cohort
   894→
   895→Segment users by how quickly they reached activation milestones.
   896→
   897→```sql
   898→-- Distribution of time-to-first-view (hours) by signup week
   899→WITH ttfv AS (
   900→    SELECT
   901→        u.id,
   902→        DATE_TRUNC('week', u.created_at)::date AS cohort_week,
   903→        EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (MIN(vi.viewed_at) - u.created_at)) / 3600 AS hours_to_first_view
   904→    FROM users u
   905→    LEFT JOIN viewed_ideas vi ON vi.user_id = u.id
   906→    WHERE u.created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '60 days'
   907→    GROUP BY u.id, u.created_at
   908→)
   909→SELECT
   910→    cohort_week,
   911→    COUNT(*) AS cohort_size,
   912→    COUNT(hours_to_first_view) AS viewed_at_least_once,
   913→    ROUND(AVG(hours_to_first_view)::numeric, 1) AS avg_hours_to_first_view,
   914→    ROUND(PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY hours_to_first_view)::numeric, 1)
   915→        AS median_hours_to_first_view,
   916→    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE hours_to_first_view <= 1) AS viewed_within_1h,
   917→    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE hours_to_first_view <= 24) AS viewed_within_24h
   918→FROM ttfv
   919→GROUP BY cohort_week
   920→ORDER BY cohort_week;
   921→```
   922→
   923→---
   924→
   925→## Appendix: Recommended Instrumentation Priority
   926→
   927→### Phase 1 (Immediate -- unblocks core analytics)
   928→
   929→1. **G1**: Add page view tracking (frontend analytics integration)
   930→2. **G2**: Add signup source attribution (UTM params on user creation)
   931→3. **G4**: Fix `viewed_ideas` to allow repeat views (or add `idea_impressions` table)
   932→4. **G5**: Fix Enterprise stats endpoint (replace hardcoded zeros)
   933→
   934→### Phase 2 (Next sprint -- improves diagnostic capability)
   935→
   936→5. **G3**: Add email click tracking
   937→6. **G9**: Add subscription lifecycle events table
   938→7. **G11**: Unify onboarding emails into `email_logs`
   939→8. **G8**: Compute and expose TTFV metric
   940→
   941→### Phase 3 (Backlog -- enriches analysis)
   942→
   943→9. **G6**: Search query logging
   944→10. **G7**: Frontend product analytics SDK
   945→11. **G10**: Materialized view for idea engagement counts
   946→12. **G12**: Link daily digest emails to specific ideas
   947→

data.prompt:
Read the file docs/audits/data-analyst-audit.md and provide a concise summary with:
1. The overall grade given (if provided)
2. A 2-3 sentence summary of the audit's findings
3. The top 5 priority recommendations (one line each)

Return ONLY the summary, no preamble.

content.text:
**Grade: B**

The ZeroToShip codebase demonstrates well-structured architecture with strong config centralization, solid error handling, and thoughtful pipeline persistence, but is hampered by incomplete migration to centralized patterns (logger, config access), a critical `as any` cast vulnerability in admin routes, and 4x duplicated Resend email-sending logic across multiple files.

**Top 5 Priority Recommendations:**

1. Eliminate `as any` casts in `src/api/routes/admin.ts` — add Zod runtime validation for JSONB `phaseResults` to prevent crashes from malformed data
2. Extract generic phase runner from `src/scheduler/orchestrator.ts` — refactor 4x repeated phase-handling boilerplate into `executePhase<T>()` helper
3. Centralize Resend email sending into `src/lib/resend.ts` — consolidate 4 independent implementations to ensure consistent retry/error handling
4. Migrate `src/lib/alerts.ts` to centralized config and Pino logger — eliminate 6x `process.env` direct access and 10x `console.*` calls
5. Move `IdeaBrief` and `UserTier` to `packages/shared/` — consolidate core types scattered across schema.ts and brief-generator.ts

content.text:
**Grade: B**

The ZeroToShip codebase demonstrates well-structured architecture with strong config centralization, solid error handling, and thoughtful pipeline persistence, but is hampered by incomplete migration to centralized patterns (logger, config access), a critical `as any` cast vulnerability in admin routes, and 4x duplicated Resend email-sending logic across multiple files.

**Top 5 Priority Recommendations:**

1. Eliminate `as any` casts in `src/api/routes/admin.ts` — add Zod runtime validation for JSONB `phaseResults` to prevent crashes from malformed data
2. Extract generic phase runner from `src/scheduler/orchestrator.ts` — refactor 4x repeated phase-handling boilerplate into `executePhase<T>()` helper
3. Centralize Resend email sending into `src/lib/resend.ts` — consolidate 4 independent implementations to ensure consistent retry/error handling
4. Migrate `src/lib/alerts.ts` to centralized config and Pino logger — eliminate 6x `process.env` direct access and 10x `console.*` calls
5. Move `IdeaBrief` and `UserTier` to `packages/shared/` — consolidate core types scattered across schema.ts and brief-generator.ts

## Overall Grade: B-

The audit found a solid security foundation with good practices in validation, ORM, webhook verification, and access control, but critical vulnerabilities in unsubscribe token predictability (IDOR) and secrets being baked into Docker images compromise the grade. High-priority issues include unvalidated admin inputs, authentication middleware control flow gaps, and direct process.env access that bypass security standards.

**Top 5 Priority Recommendations:**

1. Replace unsubscribe token with cryptographically random tokens instead of user UUIDs to prevent mass unsubscription attacks (C-1)
2. Update `.dockerignore` to exclude `.env`, `.env.*`, `web/`, `tests/`, and other sensitive files to prevent secrets in Docker images (C-2)
3. Add Zod schema validation to all admin route request bodies instead of unsafe type assertions (H-3)
4. Add explicit `return` statement after 401 response in `requireAuth` middleware for consistent control flow (H-2)
5. Replace direct `process.env` access in alerts module with validated `config` object to align with security standards (H-4)

content.text:
## Overall Grade: B-

The audit found a solid security foundation with good practices in validation, ORM, webhook verification, and access control, but critical vulnerabilities in unsubscribe token predictability (IDOR) and secrets being baked into Docker images compromise the grade. High-priority issues include unvalidated admin inputs, authentication middleware control flow gaps, and direct process.env access that bypass security standards.

**Top 5 Priority Recommendations:**

1. Replace unsubscribe token with cryptographically random tokens instead of user UUIDs to prevent mass unsubscription attacks (C-1)
2. Update `.dockerignore` to exclude `.env`, `.env.*`, `web/`, `tests/`, and other sensitive files to prevent secrets in Docker images (C-2)
3. Add Zod schema validation to all admin route request bodies instead of unsafe type assertions (H-3)
4. Add explicit `return` statement after 401 response in `requireAuth` middleware for consistent control flow (H-2)
5. Replace direct `process.env` access in alerts module with validated `config` object to align with security standards (H-4)

**Overall Grade**: C+ (numerous critical accessibility gaps; strong foundation but needs immediate remediation)

**Summary**: The ZeroToShip frontend has solid accessibility foundations (proper ARIA patterns on some components, dark mode support, skip-to-content on landing) but suffers from 14 critical accessibility failures across modals, keyboard navigation, color-only indicators, and missing landmarks. Additionally, 19 usability issues and 12 polish improvements were identified, with focus ring inconsistencies and confirmation dialogs particularly problematic.

**Top 5 Priority Recommendations**:
1. Fix archive modal and CompactCard: add dialog role, focus trap, and keyboard accessibility (C-01, C-05)
2. Add `<main>` landmark and skip-to-content link on authenticated pages (C-06, C-07)
3. Fix FeedbackModal with dialog role, focus trap, and accessible NPS labels (C-02, C-03)
4. Add radiogroup/radio semantics to settings email frequency and theme selectors (C-08, C-09)
5. Add accessible score labels to replace color-only visual indicators (C-04)

**No overall grade provided.**

ZeroToShip is a soft-launched social signal platform combining Reddit, Hacker News, and GitHub scraping with AI-powered pain point detection and automated brief generation, achieving a unique competitive position with agent-ready spec generation. The core product loop is functional in production with complete scraping, analysis, generation, and email delivery infrastructure. The product has strong market timing with the GummySearch shutdown (135K+ displaced users) but faces critical PMF validation gaps around keyword monitoring, source transparency, and free trial configuration.

**Top 5 Priority Recommendations:**

1. **Source Links on Ideas** (RICE 38.0) — Display original Reddit/HN/GitHub post links using existing `sources` data for transparency and trust.
2. **Free Trial (7-day Pro)** (RICE 40.8) — Configure Stripe free trial to unlock industry-standard conversion mechanism for the Builder tier.
3. **Keyword Monitoring & Saved Searches** (RICE 7.2) — Ship the core GummySearch refugee feature before the migration window closes (requires 3 weeks).
4. **"Why This Idea" Explainer** (RICE 28.8) — Surface signal counts and source breakdown under each idea to build confidence in AI scoring.
5. **Share Idea as Link** (RICE 11.9) — Create public shareable idea pages with OG meta tags to drive organic SEO growth and organic acquisition.

ZeroToShip is a soft-launched social signal platform combining Reddit, Hacker News, and GitHub scraping with AI-powered pain point detection and automated brief generation, achieving a unique competitive position with agent-ready spec generation. The core product loop is functional in production with complete scraping, analysis, generation, and email delivery infrastructure. The product has strong market timing with the GummySearch shutdown (135K+ displaced users) but faces critical PMF validation gaps around keyword monitoring, source transparency, and free trial configuration.

**Top 5 Priority Recommendations:**

1. **Source Links on Ideas** (RICE 38.0) — Display original Reddit/HN/GitHub post links using existing `sources` data for transparency and trust.
2. **Free Trial (7-day Pro)** (RICE 40.8) — Configure Stripe free trial to unlock industry-standard conversion mechanism for the Builder tier.
3. **Keyword Monitoring & Saved Searches** (RICE 7.2) — Ship the core GummySearch refugee feature before the migration window closes (requires 3 weeks).
4. **"Why This Idea" Explainer** (RICE 28.8) — Surface signal counts and source breakdown under each idea to build confidence in AI scoring.
5. **Share Idea as Link** (RICE 11.9) — Create public shareable idea pages with OG meta tags to drive organic SEO growth and organic acquisition.

## Summary

**Overall Grade:** Not assigned (structured as findings audit)

**Executive Summary:** ZeroToShip has a solid product foundation but leaves significant conversion gains on the table. The biggest opportunities are: social proof is completely absent, the free tier is too generous to motivate upgrades, the dashboard lacks guided onboarding for new users, and multiple CTAs compete with each other on the landing page.

**Top 5 Priority Recommendations:**

1. **1.4 – Add social proof to landing page** (testimonials, user counts, logos) — Expected +15-25% signup rate, Medium effort
2. **2.2 – Remove email confirmation gate** (allow immediate dashboard access after signup) — Expected +20-30% activation rate, Medium effort
3. **2.3 – Add first-time user onboarding on dashboard** (welcome banner, usage indicators) — Expected +10-15% spec generation, Medium effort
4. **4.7 – Server-render idea pages for SEO** (unlock programmatic SEO channel) — High impact, High effort
5. **6.1 – Tighten free tier to create upgrade motivation** (gate partial brief content or limit archive to 7 days) — Expected +5-10% free-to-paid conversion, Medium effort

content.text:
## Summary

**Overall Grade:** Not assigned (structured as findings audit)

**Executive Summary:** ZeroToShip has a solid product foundation but leaves significant conversion gains on the table. The biggest opportunities are: social proof is completely absent, the free tier is too generous to motivate upgrades, the dashboard lacks guided onboarding for new users, and multiple CTAs compete with each other on the landing page.

**Top 5 Priority Recommendations:**

1. **1.4 – Add social proof to landing page** (testimonials, user counts, logos) — Expected +15-25% signup rate, Medium effort
2. **2.2 – Remove email confirmation gate** (allow immediate dashboard access after signup) — Expected +20-30% activation rate, Medium effort
3. **2.3 – Add first-time user onboarding on dashboard** (welcome banner, usage indicators) — Expected +10-15% spec generation, Medium effort
4. **4.7 – Server-render idea pages for SEO** (unlock programmatic SEO channel) — High impact, High effort
5. **6.1 – Tighten free tier to create upgrade motivation** (gate partial brief content or limit archive to 7 days) — Expected +5-10% free-to-paid conversion, Medium effort

## CFO Audit Summary

**Overall Grade**: Not explicitly assigned (audit is analytical, not graded A-F)

**Audit Findings**: ZeroToShip has an exceptionally favorable cost structure with gross margins exceeding 95% at scale, achieved through near-zero per-user pipeline costs ($0.05-0.07/run). The business achieves break-even immediately (Month 1) across all three scenarios (bear/base/bull) due to fixed costs below $10/month at launch. However, Enterprise pricing is underpriced by 34-50% relative to market benchmarks, and a critical discrepancy exists between the codebase's annual billing structure (16.7% discount) and the Strategy doc's recommendation (35%/17%).

**Top 5 Priority Recommendations**:

1. Hold Builder tier at $19/mo through Month 6; increase Enterprise to $149/mo at Month 3-4 with Slack integration and keyword monitoring first.
2. Resolve annual pricing discrepancy: keep codebase's 16.7% industry-standard discount and update Strategy doc to align.
3. Implement the validation add-on at $79/idea immediately (schema and routes exist; near-zero marginal cost).
4. Focus on reducing monthly churn from 6% to 4% — doubles LTV and is the highest-leverage financial action.
5. Plan email scaling strategy before 500 users (sequential 1/sec sending at 5K users takes 83 minutes); upgrade to Resend Pro and batch strategies.

content.text:
## CFO Audit Summary

**Overall Grade**: Not explicitly assigned (audit is analytical, not graded A-F)

**Audit Findings**: ZeroToShip has an exceptionally favorable cost structure with gross margins exceeding 95% at scale, achieved through near-zero per-user pipeline costs ($0.05-0.07/run). The business achieves break-even immediately (Month 1) across all three scenarios (bear/base/bull) due to fixed costs below $10/month at launch. However, Enterprise pricing is underpriced by 34-50% relative to market benchmarks, and a critical discrepancy exists between the codebase's annual billing structure (16.7% discount) and the Strategy doc's recommendation (35%/17%).

**Top 5 Priority Recommendations**:

1. Hold Builder tier at $19/mo through Month 6; increase Enterprise to $149/mo at Month 3-4 with Slack integration and keyword monitoring first.
2. Resolve annual pricing discrepancy: keep codebase's 16.7% industry-standard discount and update Strategy doc to align.
3. Implement the validation add-on at $79/idea immediately (schema and routes exist; near-zero marginal cost).
4. Focus on reducing monthly churn from 6% to 4% — doubles LTV and is the highest-leverage financial action.
5. Plan email scaling strategy before 500 users (sequential 1/sec sending at 5K users takes 83 minutes); upgrade to Resend Pro and batch strategies.

**Overall Grade**: Not explicitly provided

**Summary**: This is a comprehensive data instrumentation audit of the ZeroToShip platform's current analytics capabilities and gaps. The audit maps the complete data model, defines a KPI framework anchored on Weekly Active Engaged Users (WAEU) as the north star metric, and provides SQL queries for measuring 20+ key metrics across revenue, engagement, pipeline health, and retention. While the platform has solid foundational tracking (users, subscriptions, ideas, email logs), there are critical gaps in page view tracking, signup attribution, email click tracking, and repeat view measurement that prevent complete funnel visibility.

**Top 5 Priority Recommendations**:

1. Add page view tracking (table or lightweight analytics integration) to measure landing page and explore page performance
2. Add signup source/UTM attribution columns to the users table to identify which channels drive conversions
3. Fix `viewed_ideas` to allow repeat views or create an `idea_impressions` table for accurate dwell time measurement
4. Add email click tracking to `email_logs` to measure daily digest CTR and content performance
5. Fix the Enterprise stats endpoint to query actual engagement data instead of returning hardcoded zeros
