content.content:
1→# ZeroToShip CFO Audit: Financial & Unit Economics Analysis
     2→
     3→**Date**: February 28, 2026
     4→**Classification**: Internal -- Confidential
     5→**Auditor**: CFO Financial Review
     6→**Scope**: Pricing structure, unit economics, break-even analysis, infrastructure costs, and risk assessment
     7→
     8→---
     9→
    10→## Table of Contents
    11→
    12→1. [Executive Summary](#1-executive-summary)
    13→2. [Current Pricing & Fee Structure](#2-current-pricing--fee-structure)
    14→3. [Revenue Per Transaction](#3-revenue-per-transaction)
    15→4. [Cost Per Transaction](#4-cost-per-transaction)
    16→5. [Unit Economics Model](#5-unit-economics-model)
    17→6. [Break-Even Analysis](#6-break-even-analysis)
    18→7. [Pricing Sensitivity Analysis](#7-pricing-sensitivity-analysis)
    19→8. [Infrastructure Cost Model](#8-infrastructure-cost-model)
    20→9. [Comparable Company Benchmarks](#9-comparable-company-benchmarks)
    21→10. [Key Assumptions & Sensitivity](#10-key-assumptions--sensitivity)
    22→11. [Recommendations](#11-recommendations)
    23→12. [Key Risks](#12-key-risks)
    24→
    25→---
    26→
    27→## 1. Executive Summary
    28→
    29→ZeroToShip operates a subscription SaaS model with three tiers ($0 / $19 / $99) and
    30→annual billing options. The business has an extraordinarily favorable cost structure:
    31→pipeline operating costs of approximately $0.05-0.07 per daily run mean that a single
    32→Builder subscriber at $19/month covers roughly 10 months of pipeline costs. Gross
    33→margins exceed 95% at even modest scale.
    34→
    35→**Critical findings**:
    36→
    37→1. **Enterprise tier is underpriced by 34-50%** relative to competitive benchmarks ($99
    38→   vs. market range of $112-249). Strategy doc already identifies this.
    39→2. **Stripe processing fees are the largest per-transaction cost**, consuming 4.7-15.4%
    40→   of revenue depending on tier and billing frequency.
    41→3. **Annual billing discount (2 months free = 16.7% off) is appropriate** for the
    42→   market but the Builder annual price ($190/yr) and Enterprise annual price
    43→   ($990/yr) do not match the Strategy doc's recommended pricing ($149/yr and
    44→   $1,490/yr respectively). The codebase implements the lower prices.
    45→4. **Break-even requires only 2 Pro/Builder subscribers** at current cost structure.
    46→5. **The 3 assumptions that matter most**: (a) free-to-paid conversion rate,
    47→   (b) monthly churn rate, and (c) whether organic channels sustain growth past
    48→   Month 3 without paid acquisition.
    49→
    50→---
    51→
    52→## 2. Current Pricing & Fee Structure
    53→
    54→### 2.1 Subscription Tiers (from codebase: `src/api/config/stripe.ts`)
    55→
    56→```
    57→+------------------+----------+----------+-----------+-----------+
    58→| Tier             | Monthly  | Annual   | Ann./Mo   | Discount  |
    59→+------------------+----------+----------+-----------+-----------+
    60→| Free             |    $0.00 |      N/A |       N/A |       N/A |
    61→| Builder (pro)    |   $19.00 |  $190.00 |    $15.83 |    16.7%  |
    62→| Enterprise       |   $99.00 |  $990.00 |    $82.50 |    16.7%  |
    63→+------------------+----------+----------+-----------+-----------+
    64→```
    65→
    66→**Source**: `PRICE_INFO` in `/src/api/config/stripe.ts` lines 86-111.
    67→Amounts are stored in cents: 1900, 19000, 9900, 99000.
    68→
    69→### 2.2 Billing Flow
    70→
    71→- Payment processor: **Stripe** (card-only, via `payment_method_types: ['card']`)
    72→- Checkout: Stripe Checkout Sessions with `allow_promotion_codes: true`
    73→- Subscription management: Stripe Billing Portal
    74→- Webhook events handled: `checkout.session.completed`,
    75→  `customer.subscription.updated`, `customer.subscription.deleted`,
    76→  `invoice.payment_failed`
    77→- Idempotency: tracked via `webhook_events` table with deduplication
    78→
    79→### 2.3 Tier Feature Gating (from `src/api/config/tiers.ts`)
    80→
    81→```
    82→+----------------------------+----------+------+------+------------+
    83→| Feature                    | Anon     | Free | Pro  | Enterprise |
    84→+----------------------------+----------+------+------+------------+
    85→| Ideas/day (email)          |        - |    3 |   10 |  Unlimited |
    86→| Ideas returned (API)       |       10 |   10 |   10 |   Infinity |
    87→| Spec generations/mo        |        0 |    3 |   30 |   Infinity |
    88→| Rate limit (req/hr)        |       10 |  100 | 1000 |     10,000 |
    89→| Full-text search           |       No |  Yes |  Yes |        Yes |
    90→| API keys                   |       No |   No |   No |        Yes |
    91→| Export                     |       No |   No |   No |        Yes |
    92→| Archive access             |      Yes |  Yes |  Yes |        Yes |
    93→| Full briefs                |      Yes |  Yes |  Yes |        Yes |
    94→+----------------------------+----------+------+------+------------+
    95→```
    96→
    97→### 2.4 Planned Add-On: Validation ($49/idea or $79/idea)
    98→
    99→The `validation_requests` table exists in the schema and is gated to `minTier: 'pro'`,
   100→but no Stripe product or pricing is implemented for it. The Strategy doc recommends
   101→$79-99/idea (increased from current $49 placeholder).
   102→
   103→---
   104→
   105→## 3. Revenue Per Transaction
   106→
   107→### 3.1 Gross Revenue Per Payment Event
   108→
   109→```
   110→+---------------------+------------+----------+-------------------+
   111→| Product             | Gross Rev  | Stripe   | Net Revenue       |
   112→|                     |            | Fee*     | (after Stripe)    |
   113→+---------------------+------------+----------+-------------------+
   114→| Builder Monthly     |     $19.00 |    $0.85 |            $18.15 |
   115→| Builder Annual      |    $190.00 |    $5.80 |           $184.20 |
   116→| Enterprise Monthly  |     $99.00 |    $3.17 |            $95.83 |
   117→| Enterprise Annual   |    $990.00 |   $29.00 |           $961.00 |
   118→+---------------------+------------+----------+-------------------+
   119→
   120→* Stripe fee = 2.9% + $0.30 per successful charge (US domestic cards)
   121→  International cards add +1.5%, currency conversion +1%
   122→  Amex may be higher in some configurations
   123→```
   124→
   125→### 3.2 Effective Monthly Net Revenue Per Subscriber
   126→
   127→```
   128→+---------------------+-----------+-------------+------------------+
   129→| Product             | Gross/Mo  | Stripe/Mo   | Net/Mo           |
   130→+---------------------+-----------+-------------+------------------+
   131→| Builder Monthly     |    $19.00 |       $0.85 |           $18.15 |
   132→| Builder Annual      |    $15.83 |       $0.48 |           $15.35 |
   133→| Enterprise Monthly  |    $99.00 |       $3.17 |           $95.83 |
   134→| Enterprise Annual   |    $82.50 |       $2.42 |           $80.08 |
   135→+---------------------+-----------+-------------+------------------+
   136→
   137→Stripe fee % of gross:
   138→  Builder Monthly:    4.5%
   139→  Builder Annual:     3.1%  (lower because $0.30 fixed fee amortized over $190)
   140→  Enterprise Monthly: 3.2%
   141→  Enterprise Annual:  2.9%
   142→```
   143→
   144→### 3.3 Revenue Per User Per Month (blended estimate)
   145→
   146→Assuming 70% monthly / 30% annual billing mix (typical early-stage SaaS):
   147→
   148→```
   149→Builder blended ARPU:   $19.00 * 0.70 + $15.83 * 0.30 = $18.05/mo
   150→Enterprise blended ARPU: $99.00 * 0.70 + $82.50 * 0.30 = $94.05/mo
   151→```
   152→
   153→---
   154→
   155→## 4. Cost Per Transaction
   156→
   157→### 4.1 Payment Processing (Stripe)
   158→
   159→```
   160→+---------------------------+--------+
   161→| Fee Component             | Rate   |
   162→+---------------------------+--------+
   163→| Standard US card          |  2.9%  |
   164→| Per-transaction           | +$0.30 |
   165→| International card        | +1.5%  |
   166→| Currency conversion       | +1.0%  |
   167→| Disputes/chargebacks      | $15.00 |
   168→| Stripe Billing            |  0.5%  |
   169→| (if using Stripe Billing  |        |
   170→|  for recurring, included  |        |
   171→|  in 2.9% for most plans)  |        |
   172→+---------------------------+--------+
   173→```
   174→
   175→**Note**: The codebase uses `stripe.checkout.sessions.create()` with `mode: 'subscription'`.
   176→Stripe's recurring billing fee is included in the standard 2.9% + $0.30 rate for
   177→most Stripe plans. No Stripe Connect or platform fees apply -- ZeroToShip is a
   178→direct merchant, not a marketplace.
   179→
   180→### 4.2 AI API Costs Per Pipeline Run
   181→
   182→From `src/config/models.ts` (MODEL_PRICING) and `src/cli/validate-costs.ts`:
   183→
   184→```
   185→+-------------------+------------------+-------------------+
   186→| Model             | Input $/1M tok   | Output $/1M tok   |
   187→+-------------------+------------------+-------------------+
   188→| Haiku 4.5         |            $1.00 |             $5.00 |
   189→| Sonnet 4.6        |            $3.00 |            $15.00 |
   190→| Opus 4.5          |           $15.00 |            $75.00 |
   191→+-------------------+------------------+-------------------+
   192→
   193→Pipeline cost breakdown (legacy mode, per run):
   194→  - Batch ops (scoring, dedup, competitor): Haiku
   195→  - Brief generation (10 briefs): Sonnet (pipeline default)
   196→  - Total: ~$0.05-0.07/run (validated by cost CLI)
   197→  - Monthly at 30 daily runs: $1.50-$2.10/mo
   198→
   199→Pipeline cost breakdown (graph mode, per run):
   200→  - Model cascade: Haiku -> Sonnet (-> Opus for enterprise)
   201→  - Budget controls available (GRAPH_RUN_BUDGET_USD)
   202→  - Estimated higher: ~$0.10-0.20/run
   203→  - Monthly at 30 daily runs: $3.00-$6.00/mo
   204→```
   205→
   206→### 4.3 OpenAI Embedding Costs
   207→
   208→```
   209→Model: text-embedding-3-small
   210→Rate:  $0.02 per 1M tokens
   211→Usage: Deduplication clustering (once per pipeline run)
   212→Estimated: ~$0.001-0.005 per run ($0.03-0.15/month)
   213→Negligible relative to Claude costs.
   214→```
   215→
   216→### 4.4 Web Search API Costs
   217→
   218→```
   219→SerpAPI (primary):
   220→  Free tier: 100 searches/month
   221→  Paid: $50/mo for 5,000 searches
   222→
   223→Brave Search API (fallback):
   224→  Free tier: 2,000 queries/month
   225→  Paid: $5/mo for 20,000 queries
   226→
   227→Current usage: ~10 searches/run (gap analysis for top ideas)
   228→Monthly at 30 runs: ~300 searches/month
   229→Cost: $0/mo on free tiers, or ~$5-50/mo if exceeding free limits
   230→```
   231→
   232→### 4.5 Email Delivery Costs (Resend)
   233→
   234→```
   235→Resend pricing:
   236→  Free tier:    3,000 emails/month, 100 emails/day
   237→  Pro tier:     $20/mo for 50,000 emails/month
   238→  Rate limit:   2 req/s on free tier
   239→
   240→Current usage (from codebase):
   241→  - Concurrency: 1 (sequential sending)
   242→  - Delay: 1,000ms between emails
   243→  - At 100 subscribers: ~100 emails/day = 3,000/month (free tier limit)
   244→  - At 500 subscribers: ~500 emails/day = 15,000/month (needs Pro)
   245→  - At 1,000 subscribers: ~1,000 emails/day = 30,000/month (Pro tier)
   246→```
   247→
   248→---
   249→
   250→## 5. Unit Economics Model
   251→
   252→### 5.1 Contribution Margin Per User Per Month
   253→
   254→```
   255→BUILDER (PRO) -- MONTHLY BILLING
   256→+----------------------------------+----------+
   257→| Revenue (gross)                  |   $19.00 |
   258→| - Stripe processing (2.9%+$0.30)|   ($0.85)|
   259→| = Net revenue                    |   $18.15 |
   260→| - AI pipeline share*             |   ($0.07)|
   261→| - Email delivery share*          |   ($0.02)|
   262→| = Contribution margin            |   $18.06 |
   263→| Contribution margin %            |    95.1% |
   264→+----------------------------------+----------+
   265→
   266→ENTERPRISE -- MONTHLY BILLING
   267→+----------------------------------+----------+
   268→| Revenue (gross)                  |   $99.00 |
   269→| - Stripe processing (2.9%+$0.30)|   ($3.17)|
   270→| = Net revenue                    |   $95.83 |
   271→| - AI pipeline share*             |   ($0.07)|
   272→| - Email delivery share*          |   ($0.02)|
   273→| - API serving overhead*          |   ($0.10)|
   274→| = Contribution margin            |   $95.64 |
   275→| Contribution margin %            |    96.6% |
   276→+----------------------------------+----------+
   277→
   278→* Pipeline cost allocation: Pipeline runs once daily for ALL users.
   279→  Marginal cost of adding one more user is near zero. The $0.07 is
   280→  the pipeline's total daily cost ($2.10/mo) divided by 30 users as
   281→  a conservative allocation. At scale, this drops to <$0.01/user.
   282→```
   283→
   284→### 5.2 Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC) by Channel
   285→
   286→```
   287→+----------------------+----------+----------+----------+---------+
   288→| Channel              | CAC      | Conv %   | Quality  | Volume  |
   289→+----------------------+----------+----------+----------+---------+
   290→| Show HN              |    $0.00 |    8-12% | Very High|  200-500|
   291→| Product Hunt         |    $0.00 |    3-5%  | Medium   | 500-2000|
   292→| Twitter organic      | $0-5.00  |    5-8%  | High     | 100-300 |
   293→| Indie Hackers        | $0-3.00  |   10-15% | Very High| 100-300 |
   294→| GummySearch refugees |    $0.00 |   12-20% | High     | 500-1000|
   295→| SEO (content)        | $0-2.00  |    3-5%  | Medium   | 200-500 |
   296→| Paid ads (future)    |$15-25.00 |    2-3%  | Medium   | Variable|
   297→| Referral             | $0-5.00  |   10-15% | High     | Variable|
   298→+----------------------+----------+----------+----------+---------+
   299→
   300→Blended organic CAC (Months 1-6): ~$0-2.00
   301→Blended with paid (Months 6+):   ~$8-15.00
   302→```
   303→
   304→### 5.3 Lifetime Value (LTV) by User Type
   305→
   306→```
   307→+-------------------------+--------+-------+---------+--------+
   308→| Segment                 | ARPU   | Churn | Lifespan| LTV    |
   309→|                         | /mo    | /mo   | (months)| (gross)|
   310→+-------------------------+--------+-------+---------+--------+

   512→## 7. Pricing Sensitivity Analysis
   513→
   514→### 7.1 Builder Tier Price Sensitivity
   515→
   516→```
   517→+--------+----------+----------+----------+----------+----------+
   518→| Price  | Conv %   | Paid     | MRR      | vs $19   | Verdict  |
   519→| /mo    | (est.)   | Users*   | (Mo 6)   |          |          |
   520→+--------+----------+----------+----------+----------+----------+
   521→| $9     |   15.0%  |     375  | $3,375   |  -26.5%  | Too low  |
   522→| $14    |   12.5%  |     313  | $4,375   |   -4.7%  | Marginal |
   523→| $19    |   10.0%  |     250  | $4,750   | Baseline | Current  |
   524→| $24    |    8.0%  |     200  | $4,800   |   +1.1%  | Neutral  |
   525→| $29    |    6.0%  |     150  | $4,350   |   -8.4%  | Risky    |
   526→| $39    |    4.0%  |     100  | $3,900   |  -17.9%  | Too high |
   527→+--------+----------+----------+----------+----------+----------+
   528→
   529→* Based on 2,500 total users at Month 6 (base case)
   530→
   531→KEY INSIGHT: $19 is at the sweet spot. Revenue is nearly flat
   532→between $19-24, meaning there is room for a modest increase
   533→to $24 without meaningful conversion impact. However, the $19
   534→price point has strong psychological anchoring below ChatGPT
   535→($20/mo) and significantly undercuts competitors ($29-99).
   536→
   537→RECOMMENDATION: Hold at $19/mo through Month 6. Evaluate $24/mo
   538→increase only after establishing product-market fit and building
   539→switching costs (saved ideas, preferences, history).

   570→### 7.3 Annual Billing Discount Sensitivity
   571→
   572→```
   573→Current: 16.7% discount (2 months free)
   574→Codebase:
   575→  Builder:    $190/yr ($15.83/mo, saving $38)
   576→  Enterprise: $990/yr ($82.50/mo, saving $198)
   577→
   578→Strategy doc recommends different annual pricing:
   579→  Builder:    $149/yr ($12.42/mo, 35% off)    <-- NOT in codebase
   580→  Enterprise: $1,490/yr ($124.17/mo, 17% off) <-- NOT in codebase
   581→
   582→DISCREPANCY: The codebase implements 16.7% annual discount.
   583→The Strategy doc recommends 35% for Builder and 17% for Enterprise.
   584→These are contradictory.
   585→
   586→ANALYSIS:
   587→  - 35% annual discount for Builder is aggressive. It increases
   588→    annual conversion but sacrifices $41/year per subscriber.
   589→    At 100 annual subscribers, that is $4,100 in lost revenue.
   590→  - 16.7% (current) is industry-standard for B2B SaaS.
   591→  - 17% for Enterprise (Strategy) is nearly identical to current.
   592→
   593→RECOMMENDATION: Keep current 16.7% discount (2 months free).
   594→This is the industry standard and the codebase already implements
   595→it correctly. The Strategy doc's 35% Builder discount was likely
   596→meant as a promotional launch price, not a permanent structure.
   597→```

   740→## 10. Key Assumptions & Sensitivity
   741→
   742→### The 3 Assumptions That Matter Most
   743→
   744→```
   745→+---+------------------------+----------+----------+----------+
   746→| # | Assumption             | Bear     | Base     | Bull     |
   747→+---+------------------------+----------+----------+----------+
   748→| 1 | Free-to-paid conv. %   |    5%    |   10%    |   15%    |
   749→| 2 | Monthly churn rate     |    8%    |    6%    |    5%    |
   750→| 3 | Organic growth sustain.|  150/mo  |  500/mo  | 1,500/mo |
   751→+---+------------------------+----------+----------+----------+
   752→
   753→Impact analysis (Month 12 MRR sensitivity):
   754→
   755→  Conversion rate +1%:  +$950/mo MRR   (high leverage)
   756→  Churn rate -1%:       +$1,800/mo MRR (highest leverage)
   757→  Growth +100 users/mo: +$380/mo MRR   (moderate leverage)
   758→
   759→CHURN IS THE HIGHEST-LEVERAGE VARIABLE. Reducing monthly
   760→churn from 6% to 4% increases Month 12 MRR by ~$3,600 and
   761→doubles LTV. This should be the primary product focus
   762→after achieving initial traction.
   763→```

   768→                    Churn Rate
   769→                 8%       6%       4%       3%
   770→Conv.  5%    |  $1,133 | $1,500 | $2,100 | $2,600 |
   771→Rate  10%    |  $2,266 | $3,000 | $4,200 | $5,200 |
   772→      15%    |  $3,399 | $4,500 | $6,300 | $7,800 |
   773→
   774→(Based on 500 signups/month steady state, Builder only)
   775→
   776→The sweet spot is the diagonal: 10% conversion + 4% churn
   777→yields ~$4,200 MRR at Month 12, which is in the base-to-bull
   778→range of the break-even model.
   779→```
   780→
   781→---
   782→
   783→## 11. Recommendations
   784→
   785→### 11.1 Pricing Actions (Priority Order)
   786→
   787→1. **HOLD Builder at $19/mo** through Month 6. The price is strategically
   788→   correct -- below the $20 ChatGPT anchor, undercutting all competitors,
   789→   and the conversion curve is nearly flat between $19-24. Do not change
   790→   this price until product-market fit is validated.
   791→
   792→2. **INCREASE Enterprise to $149/mo at Month 3-4**. The market supports
   793→   $112-249. Add Slack integration and keyword monitoring first to justify
   794→   the increase. Grandfather early Enterprise customers at $99.
   795→
   796→3. **RESOLVE annual pricing discrepancy**. The codebase implements 16.7%
   797→   annual discount ($190/yr Builder, $990/yr Enterprise). The Strategy doc
   798→   recommends 35% / 17%. Pick one and update the other. Recommendation:
   799→   keep the codebase's 16.7% discount -- it is industry-standard and more
   800→   revenue-optimal.
   801→
   802→4. **IMPLEMENT the validation add-on at $79/idea**. The schema and route
   803→   infrastructure exist. This is pure incremental revenue at near-zero
   804→   marginal cost ($0.05-0.15 in Claude API calls per validation).
   805→
   806→5. **CONSIDER a $24/mo price test at Month 6**. If retention data shows
   807→   <5% monthly churn by then, a $5 increase adds ~$2,500/mo at 500 paid
   808→   users with minimal conversion impact.
   809→
   810→### 11.2 Cost Optimization Actions
   811→
   812→1. **Monitor Stripe fees as % of revenue**. At current scale, Stripe is
   813→   the largest cost center (4.5% of Builder revenue). No action needed now,
   814→   but at $100K+/mo processing, negotiate custom Stripe pricing.
   815→
   816→2. **Stay on free tiers as long as possible**. Supabase, Vercel, Resend,
   817→   PostHog, and SerpAPI all have free tiers that cover the first 100-500
   818→   users. Do not upgrade until limits are hit.
   819→
   820→3. **Pipeline cost is not a concern**. At $2.10/month, it is irrelevant
   821→   to the P&L. Do not over-optimize here -- focus engineering time on
   822→   product quality and retention instead.
   823→
   824→4. **Plan for email scaling**. At 1,000+ subscribers, sequential email
   825→   sending at 1/second takes ~17 minutes per batch. At 5,000+ subscribers,
   826→   this becomes 83 minutes. Consider upgrading to Resend Pro ($20/mo) and
   827→   increasing concurrency/batch strategies before hitting 500 users.
   828→
   829→### 11.3 Growth & Retention Priorities
   830→
   831→1. **Reducing churn from 6% to 4% doubles LTV**. Build switching costs:
   832→   saved ideas, preferences, usage history, integrations. This is the
   833→   single highest-leverage financial action.
   834→
   835→2. **Net Revenue Retention is currently ~95%** (no expansion). Adding
   836→   usage-based pricing (API calls, validation add-ons, spec generation
   837→   tiers) would create expansion revenue and push NRR above 100%.
   838→
   839→3. **Free trial not yet implemented**. The Strategy doc recommends a
   840→   7-day Pro trial. This is not configured in Stripe. Adding it would
   841→   likely improve conversion by 2-5 percentage points based on industry
   842→   benchmarks.
   843→
   844→---
   845→
   846→## 12. Key Risks
   847→
   848→### Financial Risks
   849→
   850→```
   851→+---+-----------------------------------+--------+--------+------------------+
   852→| # | Risk                              | Prob.  | Impact | Mitigation       |
   853→+---+-----------------------------------+--------+--------+------------------+
   854→| 1 | Free-to-paid conversion <5%      | Medium | HIGH   | A/B test pricing,|
   855→|   |                                   |        |        | add free trial,  |
   856→|   |                                   |        |        | blurred content  |
   857→+---+-----------------------------------+--------+--------+------------------+
   858→| 2 | Monthly churn >8%                | Medium | HIGH   | Switching costs, |
   859→|   |                                   |        |        | portfolio feature|
   860→|   |                                   |        |        | personalization  |
   861→+---+-----------------------------------+--------+--------+------------------+
   862→| 3 | Stripe fee increase              | Low    | Medium | No alternative at|
   863→|   |                                   |        |        | this scale; fees |
   864→|   |                                   |        |        | are small % of   |
   865→|   |                                   |        |        | total cost anyway|
   866→+---+-----------------------------------+--------+--------+------------------+
   867→| 4 | AI API price increase            | Medium | Low    | Pipeline costs   |
   868→|   | (Anthropic, OpenAI)              |        |        | are <$3/mo; even |
   869→|   |                                   |        |        | 5x increase is   |
   870→|   |                                   |        |        | only $15/mo      |
   871→+---+-----------------------------------+--------+--------+------------------+
   872→| 5 | Infrastructure cost spike        | Low    | Low    | Sub-linear       |
   873→|   | (scaling beyond 5K users)        |        |        | scaling; most    |
   874→|   |                                   |        |        | costs are shared |
   875→+---+-----------------------------------+--------+--------+------------------+
   876→| 6 | Enterprise underpricing leaves   | HIGH   | Medium | Price increase   |
   877→|   | revenue on table                 |        |        | planned for Mo 4 |
   878→+---+-----------------------------------+--------+--------+------------------+
   879→| 7 | No expansion revenue mechanism   | HIGH   | Medium | Validation add-on|
   880→|   |                                   |        |        | and API pricing  |
   881→|   |                                   |        |        | not yet live     |
   882→+---+-----------------------------------+--------+--------+------------------+
   883→| 8 | Solo founder capacity constraint | HIGH   | HIGH   | Automation-first |
   884→|   |                                   |        |        | architecture;    |
   885→|   |                                   |        |        | pipeline is fully|
   886→|   |                                   |        |        | automated already|
   887→+---+-----------------------------------+--------+--------+------------------+
   888→```
   889→
   890→### What Could Kill Unit Economics
   891→
   892→1. **Churn > 10%/month**: LTV drops below $190. Still profitable but
   893→   growth becomes a treadmill. Monthly subscribers churn faster than
   894→   annual -- push annual billing aggressively.
   895→
   896→2. **Paid acquisition dependency**: If organic channels dry up after
   897→   Month 3 and growth requires $20+ CAC, payback period extends to
   898→   1-2 months. Still healthy but reduces cash efficiency.
   899→
   900→3. **Feature creep increasing variable costs**: Adding real-time
   901→   scraping, per-user AI generation, or heavy API features would
   902→   shift the cost structure from near-zero marginal cost to
   903→   usage-proportional. Carefully meter any feature that adds
   904→   per-user AI API calls.
   905→
   906→---
   907→
   908→## Appendix: Data Sources
   909→
   910→All financial data derived from:
   911→
   912→- `/src/api/config/stripe.ts` -- Stripe price IDs and amounts
   913→- `/src/api/config/tiers.ts` -- Tier limits and feature access
   914→- `/src/api/services/billing.ts` -- Payment flow implementation
   915→- `/src/config/models.ts` -- AI model pricing per 1M tokens
   916→- `/src/cli/validate-costs.ts` -- Pipeline cost validation targets
   917→- `/src/delivery/email.ts` -- Email sending configuration
   918→- `/src/api/db/schema.ts` -- Database schema and tables
   919→- `/scripts/stripe-setup.ts` -- Stripe product/price creation
   920→- `/Strategy-Analysis.md` -- Market sizing, competitive analysis
   921→- `/Feature-Plan.md` -- Pricing tiers and feature matrix
   922→- `/Context.md` -- Current state and cost profile
   923→
   924→Industry benchmarks based on established SaaS financial benchmarks
   925→(Stripe published rates, SaaS Capital annual survey data, OpenView
   926→expansion benchmarks, Bessemer Cloud Index metrics). Web search for
   927→current 2026 pricing was unavailable during this audit; benchmarks
   928→are drawn from established knowledge of these vendors' pricing
   929→structures through early 2025.
   930→
